summary: NoType's build/infra layer is largely unchanged from Template: gradle.properties, .editorconfig, build-config/detekt-config.yml, kover/server/kmp convention plugins, dev-scripts, init-scripts, devcontainer, and the server/core/config build are byte-identical (apart from package/rootProject renames). The real, portable deltas cluster in four areas: (1) a brand-new buildSrc EnvLoader.kt plus a :server:run doFirst env-injection that makes the committed .env authoritative over stray ambient shell vars; (2) a fully rewritten .claude/hooks/detekt-on-edit.sh that runs the standalone detekt CLI per-edited-file (auto-fix formatting + report manual issues) instead of a slow whole-build ./gradlew detekt, plus a settings.json fix from `node` to `bash` for that shell hook; (3) generic dev/CI tooling — server-watch.sh restart loop, a packaged-binary size CI gate, an Info.plist lint Gradle task, a .claude/launch.json run-config set, a .graphifyignore, and a skills-lock.json/.agents skill-vendoring lockfile; (4) small generic hardening in .gitignore (JVM crash-dump globs), version catalog (ktor-client-mock, ktor-client-websockets, composeHotReload bump), and the compose-hot-reload MCP entry in .mcp.json. Everything tied to dictation/audio/AI — the Langfuse Docker stack, OpenAI/Anthropic/Deepgram .env keys, Koog dual-stream pinning, SQLDelight/okio/whisper/concentus/llama deps, the nonWasmJs source-set restructuring, mic entitlements, and the macos-native-gate.yml — is product-specific and should not be ported as-is. Note both CI workflows are currently disabled (manual-only) for billing reasons; that change is NoType-local and must not be ported.

portableItems.name: EnvLoader buildSrc helper + :server:run .env injection
portableItems.whatItIs: A buildSrc object that parses the project .env (falling back to .env.example) into a key->value map and a resolve() that prefers .env over an ambient shell var. The server build.gradle.kts uses it in a `tasks.named<JavaExec>("run") { doFirst { EnvLoader.load(envRoot).forEach { (k,v) -> environment(k,v) } } }` so a globally-exported var (e.g. another project's LANGFUSE_*/DATABASE_URL) cannot silently shadow the committed project .env during local `:server:run`. Solves a real cross-project dev footgun that affects ANY template-based app with a .env.
portableItems.notes: EnvLoader.kt has zero product coupling — copy verbatim into Template/buildSrc/src/main/kotlin/. Then add the ~13-line `tasks.named<JavaExec>("run"){ doFirst { ... } }` block to Template/server/build.gradle.kts. No new deps. Note buildSrc/build.gradle.kts is unchanged (EnvLoader is plain Kotlin, no plugin needed).

portableItems.name: Standalone detekt CLI per-edit hook (detekt-on-edit.sh rewrite)
portableItems.whatItIs: Complete rewrite of the PostToolUse Kotlin-edit hook. Instead of running a slow whole-build `./gradlew detekt --daemon` (which can't even lint client/Compose/MVI modules since detekt is only applied to server modules), it fetches the detekt CLI + formatting plugin once (cached under .claude/hooks/.detekt/, git-ignored), derives a formatting config and a non-formatting config from build-config/detekt-config.yml, then runs two fast (~1-2s) passes on JUST the edited file: pass 1 auto-corrects formatting; pass 2 reports manual-fix issues, applying the nearest module detekt-baseline.xml. Non-blocking (always exits 0), filters known false-positives. A big editor-feedback-loop upgrade that is fully generic.
portableItems.notes: Copy detekt-on-edit.sh over Template's version. DETEKT_VERSION=1.23.8 already matches Template's libs.versions.toml detekt pin. Also apply the settings.json one-line fix: the PostToolUse command must be `bash .claude/hooks/detekt-on-edit.sh` not `node ...` (Template has a latent bug invoking a .sh file with node). Add `.claude/hooks/.detekt/` to .gitignore (NoType already did). No deps beyond java+curl on PATH, which the hook self-checks.

portableItems.name: .gitignore JVM crash-dump + detekt-cache globs
portableItems.whatItIs: Adds ignores for `hs_err_pid*.log` and `replay_pid*.log` (JVM fatal-error/replay dumps that land at repo root and should never be committed) and for `.claude/hooks/.detekt/` (the detekt CLI jars cached by the rewritten hook). Generic hardening for any JVM project.
portableItems.notes: Cherry-pick just the hs_err/replay_pid lines and the .claude/hooks/.detekt/ line. NoType also REMOVED `/kotlin-js-store/wasm/yarn.lock` because it dropped the wasm target — do NOT port that removal; Template still ships wasmJs.

portableItems.name: compose-hot-reload MCP server entry in .mcp.json
portableItems.whatItIs: Adds a `compose-hot-reload` stdio MCP server that runs `./gradlew :composeApp:hotMcpServerJvm`, letting an agent drive the running Compose Desktop app (semantic tree, click, type, screenshot) for UI verification. Generic for any Compose Multiplatform app built from the template.
portableItems.notes: Copy the compose-hot-reload block into Template/.mcp.json. Verify Template's composeApp exposes a :composeApp:hotMcpServerJvm task (it should via the composeHotReload plugin); Template currently pins composeHotReload=1.0.0 vs NoType 1.2.0-alpha01 — the MCP server task may require the newer plugin, so bump composeHotReload or confirm task availability first.

portableItems.name: .claude/launch.json run-config set
portableItems.whatItIs: A Claude Code launch.json declaring named run configurations (server, server-watch, composeApp-desktop, composeApp-web wasmJsBrowserDevelopmentRun, docker-services -> devUp, docs-static python http.server). A convenient, mostly-generic dev-launcher manifest for the template's standard surfaces.
portableItems.notes: Port the generic entries (server, composeApp-desktop-plain via :composeApp:run, composeApp-web, docker-services, docs-static). Drop/adapt the two that point at NoType-only scripts (server-watch.sh, desktop-hot.sh) unless those scripts are also ported. Rename mainClass/app references if any.

portableItems.name: Server watch-restart loop (server-watch.sh)
portableItems.whatItIs: A dev-only poll-based watch loop that recompiles + restarts the Ktor backend on source change (server/shared/core .kt/.kts plus .env and libs.versions.toml). Documents WHY true hot-reload isn't possible (embeddedServer + SuspendApp + context-receiver module lambda). Needs no external watcher (entr/fswatch). Useful for any Ktor server in the template.
portableItems.notes: Only product coupling is `APP_MAIN=com.m2f.notype.ApplicationKt` — parameterize to com.m2f.template.ApplicationKt (or read the package from settings). The find/stat logic is BSD-stat (macOS) specific; add a Linux `stat -c %Y` fallback if cross-platform dev is a goal. Put under Template/scripts/.

portableItems.name: Packaged-binary size CI/release gate (check-binary-size.sh)
portableItems.whatItIs: A shell gate that fails the build if the packaged macOS dmg exceeds a configurable MB cap (default 80). Generic pattern: bloat-regression guard after :composeApp:packageReleaseDistributionForCurrentOS.
portableItems.notes: The dmg glob path (composeApp/build/compose/binaries/main-release/dmg/*.dmg) is generic; only the comment references NoType's NFR-05. Drop the NFR-05 wording. macOS-only (dmg + BSD stat -f%z); fine as an optional desktop gate. The sibling check-idle-rss.sh is more NoType-specific (hardcodes NoType.app) — port only if you generalize the app-name.

portableItems.name: Info.plist lint Gradle task pattern
portableItems.whatItIs: A Gradle task that parses an iOS/desktop Info.plist (DocumentBuilderFactory with a no-op EntityResolver to skip the Apple PLIST DTD) and fails the build when required <key>s are missing — a CI-enforceable plist contract check. The mechanism (XML-parse-and-assert-keys) is generic even though NoType asserts the microphone key.
portableItems.notes: Extract just the lint-task skeleton (lines ~381-430 of composeApp/build.gradle.kts). Make the required-keys list a parameter/empty-by-default so Template ships the mechanism without NoType's NSMicrophoneUsageDescription assertion. Skip everything around entitlementsFile/NSMicrophone — that is product-specific (see exclusions).

portableItems.name: skills-lock.json + .agents skill-vendoring lockfile
portableItems.whatItIs: A version-1 lockfile (skills-lock.json) pinning externally-sourced Claude skills by github source + skillPath + computedHash, with the vendored copies under .agents/skills/. A generic, reproducible mechanism for pinning third-party agent skills into a repo (independent of WHICH skills — NoType happens to vendor App Store Connect skills).
portableItems.notes: Port the lockfile FORMAT/convention, not the contents — the 6 asc-* (App Store Connect) skills are iOS-publishing-specific. Ship Template with an empty `skills: {}` or only template-relevant skills. Document the install/verify flow if one exists.

portableItems.name: Version catalog: generic test/client libs + composeHotReload bump
portableItems.whatItIs: Generic additions to gradle/libs.versions.toml usable by any template app: ktor-client-mock (MockEngine-based client testing), ktor-client-websockets (generic WS client), and a composeHotReload bump 1.0.0 -> 1.2.0-alpha01. NoType also dropped ktor-client-js (because it dropped the wasm target) — do not port that removal.
portableItems.notes: Add `ktor-client-mock` and `ktor-client-websockets` aliases (both version.ref=ktor, already 3.4.0 in Template). Bump composeHotReload only if you also adopt the hotMcpServerJvm MCP entry; otherwise optional. Do NOT remove ktor-client-js from Template (Template keeps wasmJs). Skip every Koog/okio/sqldelight/whisper/concentus/jna/atomicfu/kaml/kherud-llama addition — product-specific (exclusions).

productSpecificExclusions.why: ~190 lines of docker-compose plus a root devUpLangfuse Gradle task and Langfuse-aware port handling exist to trace/manage the dictation AI cleanup/translate Koog graph (OTEL exporter, prompt management). Although 'self-hosted observability behind an opt-in compose profile' is a generic IDEA, this concrete stack is bound to NoType's AI pipeline and its keys/init are pinned to the notype project. Don't port as-is.

productSpecificExclusions.why: OPENAI_API_KEY, ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, DEEPGRAM_API_KEY, CLEANUP_MODEL_*, TRANSLATION_MODEL, *_ZDR_ACCOUNT, CLEANUP_BASE_URL/REASONING_MODEL, LANGFUSE_*, and FREE/PRO_*_CAP_MINUTES are all dictation/transcription/AI-cleanup product config. The only generic line changes are the project-name renames (OAUTH_MOBILE_SCHEME, SMTP_FROM).

productSpecificExclusions.why: sqldelight (plugin+drivers), okio, concentus (Opus codec), jna-platform, atomicfu, kaml, whisper-jni, kherud-llama, and the :core:audio/:core:database/:core:ai* modules in settings.gradle.kts are all audio-capture / on-device-STT / on-device-LLM-cleanup machinery. productSpecificExclusions.why: NoType replaced the wasmJs target with a custom nonWasmJs applyDefaultHierarchyTemplate group and bridged iosMain/androidMain so per-platform `audioPlatformModule`/STT/cleanup expect/actuals resolve. This restructuring exists only because audio/dictation modules have no wasm target. Template intentionally keeps full wasmJs support; porting this would break the template's WASM target.

productSpecificExclusions.why: Path-filtered macOS-runner gate for the JNA CGEventTap/CGEventPost/AXIsProcessTrusted/NSPanel native shims in app/dictation/impl. The path-filtered-native-gate IDEA is mildly generic, but the workflow is entirely about dictation's macOS keystroke-injection shims. Excluded (and it's also currently disabled).

productSpecificExclusions.why: Both ci.yml and macos-native-gate.yml were switched from push/pull_request triggers to workflow_dispatch on 2026-05-23 due to a GitHub Actions billing/spending-limit block. This is a NoType account-state decision; Template must keep its push/PR triggers.

productSpecificExclusions.why: Audio waveform generator, dictation MVP brief, pitch presentation, planning docs, and NoType branding skill are all product artifacts, not template infra.

risks: Ordering: port EnvLoader.kt into Template/buildSrc BEFORE adding the :server:run doFirst block, or the server build won't compile (it references EnvLoader). buildSrc/build.gradle.kts needs no change — EnvLoader is plain Kotlin.
The detekt-on-edit.sh rewrite hardcodes DETEKT_VERSION=1.23.8; it matches Template today, but if Template later bumps detekt the hook version must be kept in sync (or read from libs.versions.toml).

settings.json hook fix: Template currently runs the .sh hook with `node`, which silently does nothing. Porting the new shell-based hook REQUIRES flipping that command to `bash`, else the upgrade is inert.
Do NOT mirror NoType's .gitignore removal of /kotlin-js-store/wasm/yarn.lock or its wasm-target drop — Template keeps wasmJs; copying those would break Template's WASM build.

compose-hot-reload MCP entry depends on the :composeApp:hotMcpServerJvm task, which may require composeHotReload >= the version NoType uses (1.2.0-alpha01, an alpha). Verify task availability before bumping; an alpha plugin in the template baseline is a stability risk.
The Info.plist lint task and check-binary-size.sh are macOS/dmg-specific (BSD stat, dmg glob); port as optional desktop gates and add Linux fallbacks if cross-platform CI matters.
Do NOT port NoType's workflow_dispatch-only CI triggers — that disables automated CI and stems from a NoType billing block, not a template improvement.
skills-lock.json: port the lockfile convention with empty/curated contents — the vendored asc-* App Store Connect skills are iOS-publishing-specific and shouldn't ship in a generic template.

area: core-shared (core:models, core:mvi, core:navigation, core:testing, core:sdk, core:storage)
summary: NoType forked Template's client-core modules and kept the foundations byte-identical: core:mvi MviViewModel, core:navigation Route, the entire MVI test DSL (ViewModelTest, MviViewModelTestDsl, Statement, ViewModelTestContext, Annotations), AuthInterceptor, the apiCall() Either wrapper, AppError base groups (Auth/Validation/User/Server/Client/AI base/Group/Invitation/Document/File/Privacy), ErrorResponse, StringKey base + fromCode, ValidationSupport, UserTier/UserRole, TokenStorage, and StorageModule. The genuinely generic, portable evolutions are concentrated in three places: (1) core:sdk transport hardening in ApiClient.kt (connect-timeout cap, infinite request-timeout opt-out, WebSockets plugin keepalive, and a requireSecureBaseUrl cleartext guard) plus a much-improved ErrorMapper that captures the raw body before deserialization and supports body-code dispatch; (2) core:navigation gained a reusable cross-feature signalling/back-stack toolkit (NavSelectionSignal + navigateAdd append-only helper + navigationModule); and (3) core:testing/core:storage gained reusable test infra (in-process Ktor WebSocket test harness, a hand-rolled InMemorySettings Settings fake, and a fail-fast streaming-flow fake-builder pattern). Most other deltas (AI/Audio/Native AppError subtypes, AI_/AUDIO_ error-code mapping, SttApi/CleanupApi, hotkey/STT/cleanup/tone/HUD prefs, the dropped wasmJs target) are product-specific and must not be ported as-is.

portableItems.name: ErrorMapper raw-body capture + body-code dispatch (mapHttpError)
portableItems.whatItIs: NoType rewrote mapHttpError to read response.bodyAsText() ONCE before attempting ErrorResponse deserialization, decode the ErrorResponse from that captured string (not response.body which can only be read once), and preserve the raw body in the final fallback detail (AppError.Client.Unknown). It also adds a pluggable body-code dispatch hook (errorResponse?.let { mapXxxErrorCode(it)?.let { return it } }) so a server error envelope's `code` can override pure HTTP-status mapping. This fixes the real-world bug where third-party/provider error bodies that don't match the {code,message} ErrorResponse shape collapsed into a useless 'HTTP 400' with the actual cause discarded. This is a strict improvement to Template's mapHttpError, which only does response.body<ErrorResponse>() in a try/catch and loses the body on parse failure.
portableItems.notes: Port the mapHttpError changes (bodyAsText capture, decode-from-string with ignoreUnknownKeys, BODY_PREVIEW_CHARS truncation in the Unknown fallback) verbatim, renaming com.m2f.notype->com.m2f.template. DROP the mapAiErrorCode/mapCleanupStreamErrorCode helpers and the `errorResponse?.let { mapAiErrorCode... }` call (AI_/AUDIO_ codes are product-specific) — but KEEP the extension point as a no-op or a generic hook so future projects can plug in domain codes. apiCall() and mapException() are identical and need no change.

portableItems.name: ApiClient transport hardening: connect-timeout cap + HttpTimeout opt-out
portableItems.whatItIs: createApiClient now installs HttpTimeout with connectTimeoutMillis bounded (10s) to fail fast on half-open/SYN-blackhole connections while leaving requestTimeoutMillis unbounded (INFINITE) so long-running calls aren't cut mid-flight. Template's ApiClient installs no HttpTimeout at all, so it inherits the engine default request timeout (e.g. CIO 15s) with NO connect-phase bound — a latent foot-gun for any app with slow endpoints or flaky networks. A generic template should at minimum bound the connect phase and make the request timeout an explicit, documented decision.
portableItems.notes: Port the `install(HttpTimeout){ connectTimeoutMillis = ... }` block and CONNECT_TIMEOUT_MS const. Add `io.ktor:ktor-client-content-negotiation` is already present; no new dep needed for HttpTimeout (it's in ktor-client-core). For a generic template, prefer a SANE finite requestTimeoutMillis default (e.g. 30s) rather than INFINITE — INFINITE is NoType's product call (long LLM bodies). Make both values constructor params so forks can tune them.

portableItems.name: requireSecureBaseUrl cleartext guard
portableItems.whatItIs: A small pure helper that refuses a cleartext http://|ws:// baseUrl to any non-loopback host at client-construction time (allowing localhost/127.0.0.1/::1 and the Android emulator hosts 10.0.2.2/10.0.3.2 for dev). Turns a staging/prod misconfiguration into a fast, loud require() failure instead of silently sending plaintext. Template has no such guard — any baseUrl is accepted.
portableItems.notes: Copy requireSecureBaseUrl + LOOPBACK_HOSTS verbatim and call it at the top of createApiClient. Strip the audio/transcript wording from the doc comment (it's a generic transport-security invariant). Zero new deps.

portableItems.name: WebSockets client plugin with ping keepalive
portableItems.whatItIs: createApiClient installs the Ktor WebSockets client plugin with pingIntervalMillis (20s) to keep relays alive across NAT timeouts. Template installs no WebSockets plugin. Any future feature needing a streaming/long-lived socket (notifications, live collaboration, progress streams) needs this; making it part of the shared client is the right generic baseline.
portableItems.notes: Add `implementation(libs.ktor.client.websockets)` to core:sdk commonMain (see core/sdk/build.gradle.kts delta) and install(WebSockets){ pingIntervalMillis = ... }. The version catalog already has ktor; add the websockets alias if missing. Pure infra, no product coupling.

portableItems.name: NavSelectionSignal — app-wide one-shot pane-selection signal
portableItems.whatItIs: A tiny StateFlow-backed class (request/consume/observe a nullable String key) that lets an EXTERNAL entry point (tray menu, OS callback, deep link) ask an already-mounted shell to switch its in-shell pane WITHOUT pushing a duplicate route or touching the back stack. String-typed on purpose so core:navigation carries no feature dependency. Template's navigation module is just Route.kt; this is a clean, well-tested generic primitive for the common 'a route push can't express swap-the-current-shell's-tab' problem.
portableItems.notes: Copy class + its 4 kotest tests verbatim (rename package). Generalise the KDoc — strip the JVM-tray / macOS-AX / DashboardRoute references and describe it as 'external entry point → in-shell pane selection'. Requires kotlinx-coroutines in commonMain (NoType added it to core:navigation build.gradle.kts).

portableItems.name: navigateAdd — append-only, idempotent back-stack helper
portableItems.whatItIs: A pure Compose-free helper `navigateAdd(stack: MutableList<Route>, route: Route)` that appends a route only if it isn't already the top of the stack — making external 'open/select' callbacks idempotent and incapable of duplicating the current destination or wiping history. Encapsulates the back-stack-as-mutableStateListOf navigation pattern the template already uses, but which currently lives ad-hoc in app code.
portableItems.notes: Copy verbatim; strip the WR-04 / App.kt / tray-callback narrative from the KDoc and keep the invariant statement ('appends only, never clears, idempotent-on-top'). No deps. Pairs naturally with NavSelectionSignal.

portableItems.name: navigationModule — Koin module for core:navigation
portableItems.whatItIs: A Koin module exposing NavSelectionSignal as a singleton, included into the shared DI graph so koinInject<NavSelectionSignal>() resolves on every target. Establishes the pattern that core:navigation can own DI-provided navigation services (Template's core:navigation has no Koin module).
portableItems.notes: Port together with NavSelectionSignal. Requires adding `implementation(libs.koin.core)` to core:navigation build.gradle.kts (NoType did this). Wire navigationModule into the shared module aggregator (sharedModule).

portableItems.name: InMemorySettings — hand-rolled Settings test fake
portableItems.whatItIs: A complete in-memory implementation of multiplatform-settings' Settings interface for commonTest, backed by a MutableMap, covering all typed put/get/getOrNull methods. Avoids depending on the multiplatform-settings-test artifact (not published for every KMP target) and the unavailable MapSettings. Lets PreferencesStorage/TokenStorage be unit-tested with real round-trips on any target. Template has NO storage tests and no Settings fake.
portableItems.notes: Copy InMemorySettings verbatim (rename package). Port a SLIMMED PreferencesStorageTest that only exercises Template's existing prefs (theme, language) plus the persist-across-fresh-instance pattern; DROP the tracingConsent/hudSize/appToneMap cases (product prefs). Add kotlin-test + kotest to core:storage commonTest deps.

portableItems.name: Reactive consent/boolean-preference pattern (tracingConsent)
portableItems.whatItIs: NoType demonstrates the canonical reactive-boolean-preference recipe in PreferencesStorage: backing MutableStateFlow seeded from settings.getBoolean(KEY, DEFAULT), a var with get/set that writes-through to settings AND updates the flow, an observeXxx(): Flow<Boolean>, and a privacy-safe default-false. tracingConsent specifically is the generic 'user consent flag gating data leaving the device' pattern useful to any app with telemetry/analytics consent.
portableItems.notes: Don't port NoType's specific keys. Instead, if Template wants a baseline, add a single generic `telemetryConsent`/`analyticsConsent` Boolean pref following the exact MutableStateFlow + write-through + observe + default-false recipe (lines ~34-35, ~233 observe, ~245 key, ~290 default in the NoType file). The pattern is the deliverable, not the keys.

portableItems.name: In-process Ktor WebSocket SDK test harness + streaming fake-builder pattern
portableItems.whatItIs: Two reusable testing patterns: (a) SttApiStreamTest drives a real WS handshake against an in-process Ktor testApplication with the server WebSockets plugin (because MockEngine doesn't implement WS) — the only way to exercise client.webSocket{ incoming/outgoing } code; (b) FakeSttApiBuilder shows the fail-fast streaming-Flow fake-builder convention (default emptyFlow/Left(Unknown), opt-in `stream{}`/`streamWithFrames{}` configuration) extending the existing FakeXxxApiBuilder family. Together they establish how to test Flow-returning and WS-backed SDK APIs.
portableItems.notes: Port the jvmTest dependency block from core/sdk/build.gradle.kts (ktor-client-mock, ktor-server-test-host, ktor-server-websockets, ktor-server-content-negotiation, turbine, kotest-arrow) so the template SUPPORTS WS/streaming SDK tests. Keep the SttApiStreamTest only as a TEMPLATE/example renamed to something generic (e.g. a sample StreamingApiTest) — its TranscriptUpdate/SttStreamControl types are product-specific. The fail-fast fake-builder convention is already in Template's FakeApiBuilders; just document the streaming variant (default emptyFlow, opt-in factory) as the pattern.

portableItems.name: WebSocket→Flow streaming SDK pattern (channelFlow + closeReason mapping)
portableItems.whatItIs: SttApiImpl.openStream encodes a robust, reusable recipe for exposing a Ktor client WebSocket as a cold Flow: channelFlow, a launched sender coroutine collecting an outbound Flow, an inbound loop deserializing Frame.Text, mapping non-1000 closeReason codes to a typed terminal error event, rethrowing CancellationException, and converting other throwables via mapException. This is the generic skeleton any streaming feature would copy.
portableItems.notes: Do NOT port SttApiImpl itself (audio/PCM/Deepgram, END_OF_AUDIO handshake, AI.WebSocketDropped). Port it as a documented PATTERN or a tiny generic example showing channelFlow + sender launch + closeReason→typed-error + CancellationException handling. Depends on the WebSockets client plugin (separate portable item) being installed.

productSpecificExclusions.name: AppError.AI extensions, AppError.Audio, AppError.Native
productSpecificExclusions.why: The AI subtypes (UnsupportedSource, UploadFailed, RateLimited, PayloadTooLarge, Cleanup*/Translation* timeouts/refusals, WebSocketDropped, UpstreamError), the entire Audio group (mic permission, device, encryption/decryption, disk-full), and the Native group (accessibility-denied, injection-failed, hotkey-tap, window-bridge) are tied to NoType's dictation/audio/transcription/native-injection domain. The AppError base hierarchy and the AI BASE class already exist identically in Template; only these leaf subtypes are product-specific. (Native permission patterns could inspire a future generic desktop-permission error group, but not as-is.)

productSpecificExclusions.name: mapAiErrorCode / mapCleanupStreamErrorCode in ErrorMapper
productSpecificExclusions.why: These map the AI_*/AUDIO_* server error-code namespace to the product-specific AppError subtypes above. Port the body-code DISPATCH mechanism (the extension point) but not these concrete mappings.

productSpecificExclusions.name: SttApi/SttApiImpl + CleanupApi/CleanupApiImpl and their Sdk facade/SdkModule/FakeSdkBuilder wiring
productSpecificExclusions.why: Speech-to-text upload + WebSocket streaming and AI text-cleanup are NoType's core product surface. The Sdk-by-delegation facade pattern, SdkModule single{} registration, and FakeSdkBuilder sub-builder pattern are generic and already in Template — only the two extra API delegations/builders are product-specific.

productSpecificExclusions.name: PreferencesStorage product preferences
productSpecificExclusions.why: hotkeyMode/hotkeyBinding, translateHotkey*, translateFavorites, translateFlowEnabled, sttMode, cleanupMode, hudSize, appToneMap, preferredTerms encode dictation/translate/tone/HUD/glossary domain choices. Only the reactive-preference RECIPE is generic; theme/language already exist in Template.

productSpecificExclusions.name: Product DTOs: TranscriptDtos, TranscriptUpdate, SttStreamControl, CleanupDtos, Tone, AiDtos additions
productSpecificExclusions.why: Audio/transcription/AI-cleanup/tone payloads. The @Serializable DTO + sealed-update + control-token PATTERNS are generic, but these specific types must not be ported. New StringKey entries (ACCESSIBILITY_*, AUDIO_*, INJECTION_CLIPBOARD_FALLBACK_USED, TRAY_MOUNT_FAILED_NOTICE, SIGN_IN_BANNER_*) are likewise product UI strings — the StringKey enum mechanism + fromCode are already shared.

productSpecificExclusions.name: Dropped wasmJs target across core:sdk, core:testing, core:navigation build.gradle.kts
productSpecificExclusions.why: NoType is a desktop/mobile dictation app and removed the wasmJs (browser) KMP target. Template intentionally keeps wasmJs for genericity; do NOT remove it when porting NoType's build.gradle deltas — only cherry-pick the dependency additions (websockets, jvmTest block, koin/coroutines for navigation).

risks: Package rename: every ported file uses com.m2f.notype.*; rewrite to com.m2f.template.* (and any rootProject 'notype'→'template' coordinates). The diffs in this report were normalized with sed to PKG to expose true logic deltas.

Do NOT copy NoType's build.gradle.kts wholesale: NoType deleted the wasmJs target from core:sdk/core:testing/core:navigation. Cherry-pick only the additive deps (libs.ktor.client.websockets in core:sdk commonMain; the core:sdk jvmTest block; libs.koin.core + libs.kotlinx.coroutines in core:navigation commonMain + the commonTest kotest deps) and KEEP Template's wasmJs blocks.

ErrorMapper port ordering: the body-code dispatch hook must be added as a generic/no-op extension point, otherwise porting it verbatim drags in AppError.AI subtypes that don't exist in Template and won't compile. Port mapHttpError's raw-body capture independently of the AI dispatch.

requestTimeoutMillis = INFINITE is a deliberate NoType product decision (long LLM bodies). For a generic template choose a sane finite default and expose connect/request timeouts as constructor params, or you risk hanging UIs in forks that copy INFINITE blindly.

NavSelectionSignal + navigationModule + navigateAdd should be ported as a SET (the signal needs the Koin module to be injectable; the wire/app layer must consume requestedNavKey and call consume()). Porting NavSelectionSignal alone leaves it unwired.

WebSockets client plugin and the WS→Flow pattern are coupled: the streaming SDK pattern and the jvmTest WS harness both require install(WebSockets) on the shared client and the new ktor-client-websockets / ktor-server-test-host deps. Land the plugin + deps first.

InMemorySettings was written specifically because multiplatform-settings-test / MapSettings aren't published for all targets at the pinned version — verify the version in Template's catalog before assuming the test artifact is unavailable; if it IS available for Template's target set, prefer it over the hand-rolled fake.

area: server-core-infra (server:core:config, server:core:database, server:core:security, server/ entry point)
summary: NoType's server infra diverges from Template in a small number of high-value, mostly-generic ways plus a large product-specific surface. The genuinely generic, portable improvements are: (1) a 12-factor-correct env loader in Env.kt that walks up from CWD to find .env (fixes Gradle :server:run setting CWD to the server/ subproject) and gives System.getenv() precedence over the .env file; (2) a new buildSrc EnvLoader.kt that makes the committed .env authoritative over ambient shell vars for Gradle run tasks; (3) a generic boot-time invariant framework (BootError sealed interface + Configuration.validate(): Either<BootError, Env>) that aborts startup with operator-friendly messages; (4) a SecurityPlugin refactor that extracts a shared JWT validate block and registers both a default and a named ("auth-jwt") provider (needed for authenticated WebSockets); (5) a migration-registry dedup-by-version guard that fixes duplicate-registration crashes in test suites; (6) boot-time defense-in-depth validation + safe (length-only) key diagnostic logging in Application.kt. The database, datasource, vector, and health-check code are byte-identical aside from package renames. Everything tied to ZDR flags, cleanup/translation models, minute caps, Deepgram STT, and Langfuse config is product-specific and must not be ported as-is.

portableItems.name: Robust .env discovery + System.getenv() precedence in Env.kt
portableItems.whatItIs: Env.kt resolves the dotenv directory by walking up from System.getProperty("user.dir") to the first ancestor containing a .env file, then loads dotenv with .directory(dotenvDirectory). Fixes the real bug where Gradle's :server:run sets CWD to the server/ subproject so a project-root .env is invisible. Also flips env resolution to System.getenv(key) ?: dotenv[key] so a launch-time/platform env var overrides the committed .env (standard 12-factor behavior). Purely generic config plumbing useful to every fork.
portableItems.notes: Port ONLY lines 4 (import java.io.File), 9-19 (dotenvDirectory walk-up), the .directory(dotenvDirectory) call on the Dotenv builder, and the env() change to System.getenv(key) ?: dotenv[key]. Do NOT port any of the Ai/Stt fields, Langfuse fields, ZDR flags, or minute caps. No dependency changes needed (dotenv-kotlin already present in Template). Default mobileScheme/SMTP_FROM keep Template's "template"/"noreply@template.local".

portableItems.name: EnvLoader.kt buildSrc helper (.env-authoritative Gradle run)
portableItems.whatItIs: A buildSrc object that parses the project .env (falling back to .env.example) into a key->value Map and exposes load(projectRoot)/resolve(projectRoot,key). The server build.gradle.kts wires it into tasks.named<JavaExec>("run") { doFirst { EnvLoader.load(rootDir).forEach { (k,v) -> environment(k,v) } } } so the committed .env OVERRIDES any stray ambient shell var (e.g. a global LANGFUSE_*/AWS_*) in the spawned JVM. Pairs with the Env.kt System.getenv-first change to make .env the single source of truth for local dev. Generic: any forked project running a JVM via Gradle benefits.
portableItems.notes: Copy EnvLoader.kt verbatim into Template/buildSrc/src/main/kotlin (no package, no product refs). Add the tasks.named<JavaExec>("run") doFirst block from server/build.gradle.kts lines 16-27. Note it executes at execution time (doFirst) to stay config-cache compatible. NoType also uses EnvLoader in core/ai/cleanup-corpus and server/ai/cleanup build files (product-specific corpus/test tasks) - skip those. Consider also wiring it into composeApp/root run tasks if the template needs it client-side.

portableItems.name: Boot-time invariant framework: BootError + Configuration.validate()
portableItems.whatItIs: A generic pattern for fail-fast startup validation. BootError is a sealed interface with a human-readable .message (distinct from request-time DomainError). Configuration.validate(env): Either<BootError, Env> runs boot invariants and returns Left to abort startup; Application.module() folds it and calls error(...) on Left as defense-in-depth (so tests bypassing main() still get checked). The mechanism (sealed boot-error type + Either-returning validator + fold-to-crash at boot) is fully generic; only the specific invariants NoType checks (ZDR flags) are product-specific.
portableItems.notes: Port the SHAPE, not the content. Create a BootError sealed interface (drop MissingZdrFlag, or replace with a generic MissingRequiredEnv(name)/InvalidConfig(field, reason)). Add a Configuration.companion validate(env): Either<BootError, Env> with no-op or a couple of generic checks (e.g. JWT secret not the default in production, required URLs parseable). Wire the fold() at the top of Application.module(). Drop all ZDR/cleanup-endpoint logic and the isThirdPartyCleanupEndpoint helper (those are NoType privacy invariants). Add arrow.core (Either/left/right) import - already on Template classpath via libs.bundles.fp.

portableItems.name: SecurityPlugin named JWT provider refactor
portableItems.whatItIs: configureSecurity() was refactored to build the JWT verifier once and extract a shared validateBlock (JWTAuthenticationProvider.Config.() -> Unit), then register TWO providers: the default unnamed jwt(name = null, ...) used by authenticate { } blocks, and a named jwt(name = "auth-jwt", ...) required by authenticate("auth-jwt") { webSocket(...) }. Both share the same verifier/validator. Generic because any app needing authenticated WebSockets (or multiple named auth scopes) needs an explicitly-named provider; the no-duplication refactor is a clean improvement regardless.
portableItems.notes: Direct port - the file has no product coupling (uses jwtRealm/jwtSecret/jwtAudience/jwtDomain from Env.Auth which exist in Template). The "auth-jwt" name is arbitrary; keep it as the convention so future WebSocket routes can authenticate("auth-jwt"). Template already installs WebSockets in Application.kt (line 108), so this completes the WS-auth story. Add import io.ktor.server.auth.jwt.JWTAuthenticationProvider if not fully-qualified.

portableItems.name: Migration registry dedup-by-version guard
portableItems.whatItIs: MigrationRegistry.register() now skips a migration whose version already exists (if (migs.none { it.version == migration.version }) migs.add(migration)). The registry is a process-global mutableListOf and feature modules call registerXxxMigrations() unconditionally on every test-class setup; without the guard, repeated registrations push duplicates and migrate() violates the migrations-table primary key. Pure infra bug-fix, fully generic.
portableItems.notes: Trivial 3-line change in the register() function (Migration.kt around line 56). No deps, no product coupling. Apply verbatim.

portableItems.name: Application boot validation + safe key-length diagnostic logging
portableItems.whatItIs: Application.module() gained two infra-grade additions: (a) a defense-in-depth Configuration.validate(config.env).fold(...) that crashes boot on a Left even when tests bypass main(); (b) a startup log line that reports the LENGTHS of provider API keys (never raw values) plus boolean flags, so operators can confirm dotenv actually loaded credentials without leaking secrets. The validate-at-boot wiring and the never-log-raw-secrets length-only diagnostic are both generic operability patterns.
portableItems.notes: Port the validate().fold(...) call at the top of module() (depends on the BootError/validate port above). For the diagnostic log, port the PATTERN (log key.length + flags via SLF4J, never raw values) but swap the field set to whatever the template config exposes (e.g. googleApiKey.length, ai.enabled) - drop openai/anthropic/deepgram/ZDR fields. Skip the transcripts service injections and route installs (installTranscriptsRoutes/installSttStreamRoute/installCleanupRoute) - product-specific.

portableItems.name: Config-module test patterns (dotenv fixtures + boot-validate table tests)
portableItems.whatItIs: NoType added the first tests to server:core:config. EnvTest.kt exercises dotenv-kotlin against a temp .env fixture (round-trip, non-blank, null-on-missing) rather than fighting the process-level Dotenv captured in a private val - a reusable recipe for testing env loading. ConfigurationZdrAssertionTest.kt is a clean table of validate()-returns-Left/Right cases using kotest arrow matchers (shouldBeLeft/shouldBeRight) - a template for testing any boot-validation rule, including the host-vs-string URL comparison and trailing-slash edge case.
portableItems.notes: No build changes needed - Template's config build.gradle.kts already wires libs.bundles.testing.server + ktor json test deps (identical to NoType's). Port EnvTest's dotenv-fixture recipe but retarget assertions to a generic key (it currently asserts DEEPGRAM_* - rename). Port ConfigurationZdrAssertionTest's structure as the test for whatever generic validate() rules you keep; drop the ZDR/cleanup-endpoint cases. Uses io.kotest.assertions.arrow.core.* (already available via the server testing bundle).

productSpecificExclusions.name: Env.Ai ZDR/cleanup/translation/cap fields and Env.Stt
productSpecificExclusions.why: openaiApiKey/openaiZdrAccount/anthropicApiKey/anthropicZdrAccount/cleanupModelFree/cleanupModelPro/cleanupBaseUrl/cleanupApiKey/cleanupReasoningModel/cleanupEndpointZdrAccount/translationModel/free+pro minute caps and the entire Env.Stt(deepgramApiKey, deepgramZdrAccount) data class are NoType dictation/transcription/AI-cleanup config (DEFAULT_CLEANUP_MODEL_FREE=gpt-4o-mini, per-tier minute caps, Deepgram audio forwarding). Tied to the voice product, not generic. Template should keep its minimal Env.Ai(enabled, googleApiKey).

productSpecificExclusions.name: ZDR boot invariants in Configuration.validate()
productSpecificExclusions.why: The specific Left branches (MissingZdrFlag for openai/anthropic/deepgram/cleanup-endpoint) and isThirdPartyCleanupEndpoint() encode NoType's Zero-Data-Retention privacy/DPA commitments for forwarding user audio and transcripts to AI providers. The validate() MECHANISM is portable; these particular rules are product-specific and would crash a generic fork that has no AI keys.

productSpecificExclusions.name: Langfuse / OTel Env fields
productSpecificExclusions.why: langfuseHost/langfusePublicKey/langfuseSecretKey/langfuseEnvironment/langfuseEnabled in Env.Ai are observability config for NoType's Koog AI tracing. Per instructions, defer to the observability agent; do not port as part of server-core-infra.

productSpecificExclusions.name: Transcripts/STT/cleanup route + DI wiring in Application.kt and ServerModule.kt
productSpecificExclusions.why: installTranscriptsRoutes/installSttStreamRoute/installCleanupRoute, registerTranscriptsWireMigrations(), transcriptsWireModule include, and the TranscriptsService/CleanupService/DeepgramUpstreamFactory injections are the voice-dictation feature module wiring. Product-specific by definition (server/transcripts/* and server/ai/cleanup/* are NoType-only modules).

productSpecificExclusions.name: NoType .env.example AI/STT/Langfuse keys
productSpecificExclusions.why: OPENAI_*/ANTHROPIC_*/DEEPGRAM_*/CLEANUP_*/TRANSLATION_MODEL/FREE_*/PRO_*/LANGFUSE_* entries are the dictation product's runtime knobs. Template's .env.example should retain only the generic HOST/PORT/JWT/OAUTH/S3/SMTP/AI_ENABLED/GOOGLE_API_KEY set.

risks: Ordering: the BootError + Configuration.validate() port must land before (or with) the Application.kt validate().fold() wiring, or the entry point won't compile. Likewise the validate() port pulls in arrow.core imports - confirm libs.bundles.fp is on server:core:config (it is in both repos).

The Env.kt change has two coupled parts: the .directory(walk-up) AND the System.getenv-first precedence. Port both together - EnvLoader's Gradle injection assumes System.getenv wins over dotenv, so porting EnvLoader without the Env.kt precedence flip would have no effect at runtime.

Don't blindly copy NoType's Env.kt - it carries ~170 lines of product-specific Ai/Stt/Langfuse fields. Cherry-pick only the dotenvDirectory block + env() precedence change; keep Template's lean Ai data class.

ConfigurationZdrAssertionTest references Env.Ai/Env.Stt constructor params that won't exist in Template after you strip the product fields - retarget the test to Template's generic validate() rules rather than copying it verbatim.
SecurityPlugin's named "auth-jwt" provider is only useful once a route actually uses authenticate("auth-jwt"); harmless to add early, but document the convention so it doesn't look like dead config.

The AI-key-length diagnostic log in Application.kt logs config.env.ai.openaiApiKey.length etc. - those fields don't exist in Template's Env.Ai. Retarget to existing fields (googleApiKey.length, ai.enabled) or the port won't compile.

summary: Surprising but verified result: the four in-scope server feature modules (server:auth, server:groups, server:files, server:privacy) are byte-for-byte IDENTICAL between NoType and Template after normalizing only the package root (com.m2f.notype -> com.m2f.template) and rootProject name. I confirmed this two ways: a full recursive `diff -rq` of both normalized trees (114 Template files vs 115 NoType files) and per-file SHA checksums of representative contract/impl/repository/job/table files (all IDENTICAL). NoType added exactly ONE net-new file in scope: server/auth/impl/src/test/.../TlsTest.kt (a TLS 1.3 enforcement test). All of NoType's product coupling (transcripts data-export ExportContributor, audio/recording handling, Deepgram/Whisper) lives OUTSIDE these four modules — in server:transcripts and server:ai — so the auth/groups/files/privacy contracts, services, repositories, tables, migrations, and privacy GDPR jobs carry zero dictation-specific evolution. One generic server-build improvement exists adjacent to scope: the server root build.gradle.kts wires an EnvLoader to inject .env into the `run` task JVM (overriding ambient shell vars); Template lacks this. Because NoType has only 3 commits (fork -> rename -> v1.0 drop) and Template has MORE RECENT commits (quick-7 inline data export, quick-9 password verification) already present identically in NoType, the two repos are effectively converged on these features — there is essentially nothing in these four modules to port back that Template does not already have, except the TlsTest and the optional .env-injection wiring.

portableItems.name: TLS 1.3 enforcement test (TlsTest)
portableItems.whatItIs: A JUnit/Kotest test asserting (1) the JVM default SSLContext advertises TLSv1.3 in supportedSSLParameters.protocols (runs unconditionally, offline-safe), and (2) a real outbound HTTPS handshake negotiates TLSv1.3 (gated behind RUN_NETWORK_TESTS=true so offline CI no-ops). Generic guarantee that any Ktor outbound HttpClient (CIO/JSSE) will negotiate 1.3. Valuable as a baseline security-NFR test for ANY app making outbound HTTPS calls.
portableItems.notes: The unconditional first test (`JVM SSLContext supports TLS 1_3`) is 100% generic — port as-is. The second test hardcodes api.deepgram.com (NoType's STT vendor) as the handshake peer; for Template, replace the host with a neutral always-TLS1.3 endpoint (e.g. a known TLS1.3 host or the project's own API host) or drop the live-network test and keep only the SSLContext assertion. Rename package com.m2f.server.auth -> com.m2f.template.server.auth. Strip the NFR-12/Plan-01-06/tasks/manual-tests.md doc references (NoType-internal). Lives in auth/impl test sourceset which already has Kotest + junit on the classpath, so no new deps.

portableItems.name: EnvLoader .env injection for server `run` task
portableItems.whatItIs: buildSrc EnvLoader object + a `tasks.named<JavaExec>("run")` doFirst block in server/build.gradle.kts that parses the project .env (falling back to .env.example) and injects each key into the spawned JVM via JavaExec.environment(k,v), so the committed .env OVERRIDES any ambient shell var of the same name during local `:server:run`. Solves the silent-shadowing problem where a globally-exported var from another project leaks into the dev server. Fully generic dev-ergonomics fix for any Ktor server started via Gradle.
portableItems.notes: EnvLoader.kt is zero-coupling (only java.io.File) — copy verbatim into Template buildSrc; it likely also belongs to the build-tooling focus area, coordinate to avoid double-porting. The server/build.gradle.kts change is a ~13-line doFirst block referencing rootProject.projectDir; port it but DO NOT port the adjacent `implementation(projects.server.transcripts.wire)` line (product-specific). Comment block in the build file mentions a Briiks LANGFUSE_* example — genericize the comment. Execution-time only (doFirst), configuration-cache safe.

productSpecificExclusions.name: server:transcripts module + its privacy ExportContributor
productSpecificExclusions.why: NoType's dictation domain (transcript storage, transcript data-export contributor for GDPR). It is a SEPARATE module, not part of server:privacy itself; the in-scope privacy module's ExportContributor SPI is already generic in Template, only the concrete transcript contributor is product-specific.

productSpecificExclusions.name: server:ai module (Deepgram/Whisper STT, AI text cleanup)
productSpecificExclusions.why: Voice transcription and AI cleanup vendor integrations — pure product domain, out of the four in-scope modules entirely.

productSpecificExclusions.name: `implementation(projects.server.transcripts.wire)` in server/build.gradle.kts
productSpecificExclusions.why: Wires the product-specific transcripts feature into the server; must not be ported when copying the generic EnvLoader run-task block from the same file.

productSpecificExclusions.name: Deepgram host + NFR-12/Plan doc references in TlsTest
productSpecificExclusions.why: api.deepgram.com is NoType's STT vendor and the NFR-12/Plan-01-06/manual-tests.md citations are NoType-internal planning artifacts; replace/strip when porting the test.

risks: Primary risk is a NON-finding being mistaken for incomplete analysis: the four in-scope modules are genuinely identical (verified by full recursive diff + SHA checksums after package normalization), so there is almost nothing to port here. Reviewers expecting a long list of backend improvements should know the real backend evolution happened in out-of-scope modules (transcripts, ai) and in core/* modules (the quick-7 inline export and quick-9 password-verification commits in Template's log touch core, not these feature contracts).

Directionality caveat: Template has MORE RECENT commits than NoType and already contains content identical to NoType for these features, implying prior convergence/back-porting already occurred. Confirm with the orchestrator that Template is the up-to-date baseline before assuming any delta.

TlsTest live-network test will FAIL or hang in offline CI if the RUN_NETWORK_TESTS gate is removed during porting — keep the gate. Also the strict `== TLSv1.3` assertion can flake against peers that downgrade; the chosen handshake host for Template must reliably support TLS 1.3.

EnvLoader port ordering: copy EnvLoader.kt into Template buildSrc BEFORE adding the server/build.gradle.kts run-task block, or the build script won't compile (unresolved reference). Coordinate with the build-tooling focus area which likely also claims EnvLoader.kt.

summary: NoType's "Aurora" design system is a structural fork of Template's "Terminal" system: same architecture (CompositionLocal-based theme object, @Immutable token data classes with staticCompositionLocalOf + Unspecified defaults, Foundation-only components, custom ripple via IndicationNodeFactory, multi-mode @Preview annotation, contract of TerminalTheme.<group>.<prop>). The migration to replace Terminal with Aurora is largely a rename (Terminal* -> Aurora*, com.m2f.template -> com.m2f.notype) PLUS additive improvements: two whole new token groups (glows, motion), an expanded color palette (4-tier text + neon trio + 3rd elevation + borderStrong), a real 3-family typography system (display/body/mono with a 10-step semantic scale, while keeping legacy names re-pointed for back-compat), and a set of new generic components (segmented control, range slider, modal sheet, nav rail, chips input, kbd combo, mode badge, brand logo) alongside product-specific voice/HUD components. The token data-class shapes are nearly identical, so the theme API surface is forward-compatible: every Template screen that calls Terminal* primitives needs only a mechanical rename to compile against Aurora, and would then re-skin automatically. Aurora dropped the wasmJs target in the designsystem module and the terminal-design-* Pencil skill trio (replaced by a product-branded notype-design skill). The biggest generic wins to port are: glow + motion token groups, the multi-family typography upgrade, the neon-trio/elevation color extensions, the auroraBorder/auroraGlow/selectedCyan effect modifiers, and ~6 net-new generic components.

portableItems.name: Glows token group (AuroraGlows / AuroraGlow + auroraGlow modifier)
portableItems.whatItIs: A brand-new theme token group with no Terminal equivalent: an @Immutable AuroraGlow(color, blur, spread) record and an AuroraGlows(cyan, violet, magenta) bundle, wired into the theme as TerminalTheme.glows. Paired with Modifier.auroraGlow(glow, shape) which renders a soft colored outer shadow. Generic mechanism for any 'emphasis glow' design language; values are the only product-tinted part.
portableItems.notes: Port the data classes + LocalXxxGlows + the auroraGlow modifier and add LocalTerminalGlows to TerminalTheme. The default cyan/violet/magenta colors are Aurora-branded — keep the structure, let each project supply its own glow colors (or seed neutral values). Rename Aurora->Terminal, package notype->template.

portableItems.name: Motion token group (AuroraMotion)
portableItems.whatItIs: A new @Immutable AuroraMotion(durQuickMs, durBaseMs, durSlowMs, easeOut, easeInOut) token group wired as TerminalTheme.motion. Terminal had NO motion tokens at all — animation durations/easings were hardcoded per-component. This centralizes them. Fully generic; the only motion design system any app needs.
portableItems.notes: Drop-in: copy the file, rename, add LocalTerminalMotion provider line to TerminalTheme and the accessor. The doc-comment already flags the missing reduced-motion flag as a TODO — consider adding a reducedMotion Boolean to the token while porting.

portableItems.name: Multi-family typography system (display/body/mono + semantic scale)
portableItems.whatItIs: Terminal typography is a single JetBrains Mono family with size-only styles (xxs..xxl). Aurora upgrades to THREE families (displayFamily=Space Grotesk, bodyFamily=Manrope, monoFamily=JetBrains Mono) and a real semantic scale (display1/display2/h1/h2/h3/body/bodySm/caption/eyebrow/mono) with proper line-heights and letter-spacing. Crucially it KEEPS the legacy style names (xxs/xs/sm/base/md/xxl/chartAxis) re-pointed onto the new families, so existing call sites compile unchanged. This back-compat-preserving expansion pattern is the genuinely valuable, reusable idea.
portableItems.notes: The 3-family + semantic-scale STRUCTURE is generic and worth adopting. The specific fonts (Space Grotesk, Manrope) are Aurora-branded and ship as extra .ttf files in composeResources/font — Template only ships JetBrains Mono. To port: either (a) add the new fonts + Res references, or (b) adopt the structure but default displayFamily/bodyFamily to the project's chosen fonts. Keep the legacy-names-preserved trick regardless — it is what makes a theme swap non-breaking.

portableItems.name: Extended color palette (4-tier text, neon trio, 3rd elevation, borderStrong, fgOnNeon)
portableItems.whatItIs: Aurora adds 7 color slots Terminal lacks: textFaint (a 4th foreground tier for kbd '+', drag handles, eyebrows), bgElev2 (a 3rd elevation), neonCyan/neonCyanSoft/neonViolet/neonMagenta (accent trio for fills/glows/gradients), fgOnNeon (ink on neon fills), and borderStrong (strong hairline). The 4-tier text hierarchy, the 3rd elevation, the on-accent ink slot, and borderStrong are generic structural improvements; the neon trio is the brand identity.
portableItems.notes: Add textFaint, bgElev2, borderStrong, fgOnNeon to TerminalColors (generic, raises the ceiling for any product). The neon trio is product accent color — keep the SLOTS (e.g. accent2/accent3) but let each project pick hues. Both light+dark variants must be updated, plus the LocalXxxColors Unspecified defaults. Note Aurora made DARK the canonical theme (deep midnight #07080C) vs Terminal's lighter grays — that palette is a brand choice, not portable.

portableItems.name: Brand-stroke effect modifiers (auroraBorder / auroraStaticBorder / selectedCyan / PulseDot / auroraBrush)
portableItems.whatItIs: A modifier toolkit with no Terminal equivalent: Modifier.auroraBorder (animated rotating conic-gradient ring), auroraStaticBorder (static variant), Modifier.selectedCyan (a reusable 'active = accent' selection recipe: translucent fill + hairline border + optional glow), PulseDot (live/now indicator), and auroraBrush (gradient ink for headlines). The internal ringPath()/drawSweepRing() helpers solve a real rotation-invariant-coverage bug (documented). The selection-recipe modifier and the conic-ring math are generic UI infrastructure.
portableItems.notes: selectedCyan generalizes to selectedAccent(color=theme.accent). auroraBorder/PulseDot/auroraBrush are generic given a colors list; their defaults pull the neon trio so a Template port should default to theme accent colors. The ringPath/drawSweepRing helpers are pure geometry — port verbatim. Depends on the glows token group being ported first (auroraGlow).

portableItems.name: AuroraSegmentedControl (pill segmented single-select)
portableItems.whatItIs: A net-new generic component: a pill-shaped segmented control for single-select among 2+ options, built on Foundation selectableGroup + selectable with Role.RadioButton (keyboard + a11y parity with radios). Reads all styling from theme, introduces no new tokens. Any app needs a segmented control.
portableItems.notes: Uses auroraGlow on the active dot — either port glows first or drop the glow on the active segment. Otherwise a clean rename-only port.

portableItems.name: AuroraRangeSlider (single-value 0..1 slider)
portableItems.whatItIs: A net-new generic component Terminal lacked entirely: a Foundation-only single-value slider (Canvas track/knob + pointer drag + tap-to-position) with labeled min/max ends. Generic form control.
portableItems.notes: Cyan knob/halo color is brand; parameterize the accent color. The min/max label examples in the docstring (Casual<->Formal) are product copy, not code. Rename-only otherwise.

portableItems.name: AuroraModalSheet (scrim + rising centered sheet)
portableItems.whatItIs: A net-new generic overlay component: a dimming scrim that dismisses on backdrop tap + a centered sheet that rises in (opacity + translateY + scale). Foundation-only, theme-driven, maxWidth param. Every app needs a modal sheet.
portableItems.notes: Scrim alpha and rise animation are generic. Rename-only port; benefits from the motion token group for durations but currently hardcodes 240ms.

portableItems.name: AuroraChipsInput (multi-value chip entry field)
portableItems.whatItIs: A net-new generic input: a FlowRow of removable chips + a BasicTextField for adding entries (tags, recipients, keywords). Foundation-only, theme-driven. Common form control absent from Terminal.
portableItems.notes: Rename-only port. No product coupling in the mechanism.

portableItems.name: AuroraKbdCombo (keyboard chord row)
portableItems.whatItIs: A net-new display component: renders a keyboard chord as a row of AuroraKbd chips joined by mono '+' separators using the textFaint tier. Generic complement to the existing Kbd component; useful for any app showing shortcuts.
portableItems.notes: Depends on TerminalKbd (already exists in Template) + the textFaint color slot (port that color first). Rename-only otherwise.

portableItems.name: AuroraNavRail (vertical sidebar nav rail)
portableItems.whatItIs: A net-new navigation component: a 248dp vertical nav rail with brand header, scrollable items, hover states, WCAG >=44dp hit targets, selectable semantics. Generic app shell chrome any multi-screen app uses for desktop/tablet sidebars.
portableItems.notes: Hardcodes brand-specific items/icons (AuroraBrandLogo, HomeIcon, LibraryIcon). Port the rail container/item structure as generic; make the logo slot and item list caller-supplied rather than baked in. The whole 'navigation' component package is new in Aurora.

portableItems.name: AuroraDiffEventRow (semantic diff/change row)
portableItems.whatItIs: A row component with Success/Warning/Error variants mapping to semantic theme color pairs, for showing add/change/remove events. Built on existing semantic color tokens. Generic for any diff/audit/changelog UI (NoType uses it for AI-cleanup diffs but the component itself is domain-neutral).
portableItems.notes: No voice/audio coupling — it is a semantic-colored row. Rename-only port. Lives in the data component package alongside the existing chart/table/list family.

portableItems.name: AuroraModeBadge (status/mode pill with lamp)
portableItems.whatItIs: A two-variant badge (info-themed / success-themed with a status lamp) announcing a runtime mode. NoType uses it for cloud vs on-device, but the pattern (a labeled status pill with a state lamp) is generic for any 'environment / connection / mode' indicator.
portableItems.notes: The Cloud/OnDevice variant names + copy are product-specific; generalize to e.g. Info/Active or make variants caller-driven. The pill+lamp mechanism is generic. Rename-only structurally.

portableItems.name: Multi-mode @Preview annotation (adds Desktop-dark)
portableItems.whatItIs: AuroraPreview improves on TerminalPreview by adding a 4th preview (Desktop + dark mode) alongside Light/Dark/Desktop-light. Small but generic dev-ergonomics win for previewing components across all surface modes.
portableItems.notes: One-line addition: add the 4th @Preview(uiMode=NIGHT_YES, widthDp=1024) entry to Template's TerminalPreview. Trivial.

portableItems.name: LocalTitleBarInset (overlay-titlebar top inset)
portableItems.whatItIs: A staticCompositionLocalOf<Dp> (default 0.dp) reserving top space for an overlay/transparent window title bar (e.g. macOS full-window-content + traffic lights) so content offsets to clear it while full-bleed backgrounds ignore it. Generic desktop-window concern for any Compose Desktop app using a transparent title bar.
portableItems.notes: Defaults to 0.dp so it is inert on every platform until the desktop entry provides a real value — safe to port as-is. Only the JVM desktop entry point that supplies the real height is product-side. Rename-only.

portableItems.name: Expanded radius scale (adds xl=28dp, friendlier curve)
portableItems.whatItIs: Aurora adds an 'xl' radius slot (28dp for modals) on top of Terminal's none/xs/sm/md/lg/pill/full, and shifts the whole scale to generous rounded values (xs 6 vs 2, sm 10 vs 4, etc). Adding the xl slot is a generic structural extension; the rounder values are an aesthetic choice.
portableItems.notes: Port the extra xl slot to TerminalRadius (structural). Keep Template's own radius VALUES unless adopting Aurora's rounder look wholesale. Borders/Shadows token groups are byte-identical between the two systems — no port needed there.

productSpecificExclusions.name: AuroraRecordingHudPill + HudPillSurface
productSpecificExclusions.why: The dictation HUD pill (waveform + elapsed + listening/transcribing/injecting states) and its glassy blurred surface are NoType's core voice-capture chrome. Bound to recording lifecycle states and the product's signature surface.

productSpecificExclusions.name: RecordingPillWave + AuroraWaveform + AuroraBrandWaveform
productSpecificExclusions.why: Animated voice-amplitude waveform rendering — the product's explicit 'visual heartbeat' brand motif tied to audio capture. Not meaningful outside a voice/audio product.

productSpecificExclusions.name: HudIcons (CaptureWave, mic/stop/etc Canvas glyphs)
productSpecificExclusions.why: Hand-drawn icon set specifically for the recording HUD and capture surfaces; their existence is driven by the dictation feature.

productSpecificExclusions.name: AuroraBrandLogo
productSpecificExclusions.why: NoType's brand mark (stacked-dot speech glyph). Brand identity, not reusable.

productSpecificExclusions.name: notype-design skill (.claude/skills/notype-design)
productSpecificExclusions.why: Product-branded design skill (Aurora tokens CSS, NoType UI kits, voice-first tone/copy rules, brand motifs). It is the NoType analogue of Template's terminal-design-generator/implementer/sync trio and should NOT replace those generically; at most the SKILL pattern is reusable but the content is product-specific.

productSpecificExclusions.name: aurora-design-request.md
productSpecificExclusions.why: A NoType-specific design brief enumerating product screens (CapNote, DictationHud, voice settings, privacy/processor disclosure). Domain document, not template material.

productSpecificExclusions.name: Neon trio palette + dark-canonical surfaces + Space Grotesk/Manrope fonts as DEFAULTS
productSpecificExclusions.why: The specific cyan/violet/magenta accents, midnight #07080C surfaces, and chosen display/body fonts are Aurora's brand identity. Port the SLOTS/structure, not these literal values, unless the intent is to literally rebrand Template to Aurora.

risks: Ordering: port token groups BEFORE components. glows must land before auroraGlow/selectedCyan/AuroraSegmentedControl; the textFaint color slot before AuroraKbdCombo; bgElev2/borderStrong/fgOnNeon before any component referencing them. Otherwise ports won't compile.

Decide the rebrand intent up front. If the goal is literally REPLACE Terminal with Aurora in Template, it is a near-mechanical rename (Terminal*->Aurora*, com.m2f.template->com.m2f.notype names dropped) plus adding the new files — every existing Template screen (LoginScreen, DashboardScreen, ProfileScreen, AdminPanelScreen, PrivacySettings, ConsentGate, etc. — ~20 feature files listed) calls Terminal* primitives and will need the same rename to compile, after which they re-skin automatically because the token contract is preserved. If instead the goal is to harvest GENERIC improvements into the existing Terminal system, keep Terminal naming and add slots/components with neutral defaults.

wasmJs regression: NoType DROPPED the wasmJs target from the designsystem module (no wasmJsMain). Template still targets wasmJs. Do NOT copy NoType's build.gradle wholesale — keep Template's wasmJs target and provide wasmJs actuals for ImagePicker/ImageDecoder (Template already has them).

Back-compat trick is load-bearing: AuroraTypography keeps the legacy style names (xxs/xs/sm/base/md/xxl/chartAxis) re-pointed onto new families so old call sites compile. Any port of the typography upgrade MUST preserve this aliasing or it becomes a breaking change across every screen.

Font assets: the typography upgrade needs Space Grotesk + Manrope .ttf files added to composeResources/font and the generated Res font references; forgetting the assets yields unresolved references. Alternatively adopt the 3-family STRUCTURE but keep Template's single font as the default for all three families.
Borders and Shadows token groups are byte-identical between the two systems — do not waste effort 're-porting' them; only Radius (extra xl slot) and Colors/Typography (added slots) actually changed.

The Pencil design-mapping skills diverge: Template has terminal-design-generator/implementer/sync that enforce a 1:1 .pen<->Kotlin token mapping (manifest.json/REFERENCES.md/CODE-MAP.md). NoType replaced this with a CSS/HTML-kit notype-design skill (no .pen sync). If Aurora replaces Terminal in Template, the terminal-design-* skills and terminal_design_system.pen will be stale and need re-authoring for Aurora tokens, or the CLAUDE.md design-mapping section becomes incorrect.

summary: NoType added an entire Langfuse + OpenTelemetry observability stack that does not exist in Template at all (Template's `server/ai` is the generic impl/contract/wire module with agents/rag/structured and NO tracing, NO Langfuse, NO eval harness; Koog is 0.6.2 vs NoType's 1.0.0-preview7). The stack lives ENTIRELY inside the product module `server/ai/cleanup` and splits cleanly into two layers: (1) GENERIC, reusable observability infrastructure — a process-lifetime OTel SDK + custom Koog `SpanAdapter` that fixes Koog's flat-trace and `output: undefined` bugs, a two-environment trace-privacy redaction gate, a thin Langfuse Public-REST client (prompts/scores/datasets, no official Kotlin SDK exists), a cache-with-fallback prompt provider + background refresher, and an LLM-as-judge + promotion-gate eval harness — all heavily unit-tested with MockEngine/fakes; and (2) PRODUCT-SPECIFIC prompt content (cleanup/translate system prompts, the `cleaned`/`translated` structured envelope, tone/translate domain). Both halves already map onto two pre-existing global skills (`koog-langfuse-tracing`, `langfuse-prompt-management`), so the skills capture the know-how; what's missing in Template is the packaged CODE. Recommendation: extract the generic layer into a NEW `server:core:observability` module (depends only on `server:core:config` + koog-agents-otel + the Java OTLP exporter + ktor-client), move the Langfuse Env fields + version-catalog deps + .env.example + self-hosted docker profile into Template, and leave the cleanup prompts/catalog/envelope in the product.

portableItems.name: Process-lifetime OTel SDK + NonClosingOpenTelemetry wrapper (Koog flat-trace fix)
portableItems.whatItIs: The single most valuable and hard-won piece. PostProcessProvider builds the OpenTelemetry SDK ONCE per process (not per agent), wires the standard Java OTLP/HTTP span exporter through a SIMPLE span processor (Koog's KMP batch processor never drains on a long-lived server), registers a JVM-exit shutdown hook, and wraps the SDK in a NonClosingOpenTelemetry delegate whose shutdown()/forceFlush() are no-ops. Each per-call agent does setSdk(shared) + setShutdownOnAgentClose(true): the per-call SdkMeterProvider is released (no leak) but the shared span pipeline survives, so the end-of-run spans (last node, __finish__, strategy, invoke/create agent) drain instead of losing the close race. This is the generic fix for the flat/partial trace tree on ANY graph shape. Reproduce the OTel resource attributes (service.name/version/instance.id + os.*) since setSdk makes Koog's resource construction inert.
portableItems.notes: buildProcessLifetimeSdk + NonClosingOpenTelemetry are 100% generic OTel/Koog plumbing — extract verbatim into server:core:observability as e.g. KoogOtelExporter / ProcessLifetimeOtelSdk taking a generic OtelExporterConfig(host, headers, serviceName, serviceVersion). Strip: SERVICE_NAME='notype-postprocess' (make a param), the LANGFUSE_OTLP_TRACES_PATH default (param). The basicAuth() helper is generic. Keep the Java OTLP exporter dep (io.opentelemetry:opentelemetry-exporter-otlp:1.61.0) pinned to the Koog OTel SDK line. Already documented in the koog-langfuse-tracing skill SKILL.md as the verified fix.

portableItems.name: Custom Langfuse SpanAdapter (trace.output + per-generation decomposition)
portableItems.whatItIs: Drop-in replacement for Koog's internal LangfuseSpanAdapter (which is `internal` and single-instance so it can't be wrapped/stacked). It propagates trace attributes onto every span, decomposes gen_ai input/output messages into the indexed gen_ai.prompt.{i}/gen_ai.completion.{i} attributes Langfuse renders, mirrors langgraph step/node metadata, and — the key add — lifts the last inference completion to langfuse.trace.output on BOTH the root CREATE_AGENT span AND the inference span (self-hosted Langfuse v3 only reliably ingests the inference span). Encodes a verified Langfuse-v3 gotcha: promptVersion must go through freeform metadata, not the reserved langfuse.prompt.version int field, or the strict worker rejects the whole observation.
portableItems.notes: The skill koog-langfuse-tracing ALREADY ships a generified version at references/TraceIoLangfuseSpanAdapter.kt — port THAT into server:core:observability rather than the NoType class. Product-specific bits to strip: unwrapStructured() (knows {cleaned}/{translated} keys), promptIdentityForCompletion coupling, and buildPostProcessTraceAttributes' tone/language-pair metadata. Generalize the trace-output extraction to a pluggable (lastCompletion: String) -> String finalizer with identity default. emitTraceContent gating stays generic.

portableItems.name: Two-environment trace-privacy redaction gate
portableItems.whatItIs: A pure, unit-tested redaction policy: traceContentAllowed(environment, consent) = environment != 'production' || consent. In dev (and consented prod) full fidelity flows; in prod-without-consent only metadata (cost, latency, prompt version, session/user, langs) is emitted and the raw transcript (langfuse.trace.input/output) is OMITTED and per-generation content masked (setVerbose(false) -> HIDDEN:non-empty). This is a generic PII-redaction pattern for any app that traces user content to an external observability backend under a zero-retention/consent regime.
portableItems.notes: traceContentAllowed + the test are fully generic — port verbatim. buildPostProcessTraceAttributes is product-coupled (ToneId, cleanup/translate names, sourceLang/targetLang) — port the SHAPE (always-on metadata + content gated on contentAllowed) as a generic TraceAttributes builder taking (sessionId, userId, traceName, input, contentAllowed, extraMetadata: Map). The LANGFUSE_PROD_ENVIRONMENT constant is generic.

portableItems.name: LangfuseRestClient — shared authenticated Public-REST handle
portableItems.whatItIs: Thin Ktor-client wrapper over the Langfuse Public REST API: owns base URL, the precomputed HTTP-Basic base64(pk:sk) auth header, a shared lenient Json, and three primitives getText/postJson/patchJson that each throw on non-2xx (method+path+status). There is NO official Langfuse Kotlin/JVM SDK, so this is the one auth/encode/error-handling seam every domain client builds on. Completely product-agnostic.
portableItems.notes: Port verbatim into server:core:observability.langfuse. No product coupling at all. The DI note (must inject a client with BOUNDED timeouts — these are background/eval calls, never the hot path) is a generic rule worth keeping in docs. Documented in langfuse-prompt-management skill.

portableItems.name: LangfusePromptClient / ScoresClient / DatasetClient — REST domain clients
portableItems.whatItIs: Three small clients on top of LangfuseRestClient covering the full Langfuse prompt-management + eval REST surface: PromptClient (GET v2/prompts/{name}?label=, POST v2/prompts seed, PATCH versions/{v} label-promotion, with text-prompt parsing that rejects chat-array shapes); ScoresClient (POST /api/public/scores NUMERIC + CATEGORICAL); DatasetClient (POST v2/datasets + dataset-items for a regression/golden set). These exactly match the wire shapes Langfuse expects, captured by MockEngine tests.
portableItems.notes: Port verbatim. PromptSpec/PromptConfig (name, version, system, model, temperature) are generic value types — move with them. Only the PROMPTS_PATH/SCORES_PATH/DATASETS_PATH constants are Langfuse-API constants, not product. These plus their MockEngine tests are the canonical implementation behind langfuse-prompt-management skill — bring the tests too (they double as the wire-shape contract).

portableItems.name: PromptProvider abstraction + LangfusePromptProvider (hot-path-free cache w/ fallback) + PromptRefreshScheduler
portableItems.whatItIs: The runtime prompt-serving contract with three safety properties enforced in code+tests: (1) hot-path-free — specFor() only reads an AtomicReference, NEVER fetches, so the latency budget is safe; (2) stale-on-error — a failed refresh keeps last-good (map only overwritten on success); (3) fallback-to-constant — any uncached name resolves through a ConstantPromptProvider floor. PromptRefreshScheduler runs an injected-dispatcher SupervisorJob that boot-prefetches then refreshes every interval off the hot path, with a JVM shutdown hook to cancel. ConstantPromptProvider is the zero-dependency default so a keyless deploy behaves byte-identically.
portableItems.notes: The PromptProvider/LangfusePromptProvider/PromptRefreshScheduler mechanism is fully generic. The ONLY coupling is ConstantPromptCatalog (the concrete cleanup/translate prompt set) — make the catalog a pluggable interface (Set<PromptSpec> the provider iterates in refresh()) so the template ships the mechanism and the product supplies its own catalog. LangfusePromptProvider iterates ConstantPromptCatalog.all in refresh() — parameterize that. The dispatcher-injection (never Dispatchers.IO directly, Detekt InjectDispatcher) is a generic project rule.

portableItems.name: Eval / LLM-as-judge + PromotionGate harness
portableItems.whatItIs: A reusable safe-iteration harness: a JudgeModel seam (KoogJudgeModel uses Koog native executeStructured + StructureFixingParser to return a @Serializable JudgeVerdict{pass,score,reason}), a JudgeSuite that runs judges over a sample, summarizeExperiment() that aggregates per-judge mean/passRate/allPass into an ExperimentSummary, and PromotionGate.shouldPromote(corpusPassed, experiment, minMeanScore) + PromptPromoter that moves the `production` label via REST ONLY when corpus + every judge + score-threshold clear. The aggregation/gate logic is pure and unit-tested with a fake JudgeModel; the gate truth-table is the door that reconciles 'iterate fast' with 'no regression ships'.
portableItems.notes: Generic, port as-is: PromotionGate, PromptPromoter, ExperimentSummary/JudgeAggregate/summarizeExperiment, JudgeVerdict, JudgeSuite, JudgeModel seam, KoogJudgeModel, allPass(). NOT generic: the Judge enum's four hardcoded systemPrompts (Faithfulness/InjectionResistance/NoTranslationLeak/FormattingQuality) and EvalSample's fields (raw/cleaned/finalText/translated/langs) are dictation-domain — refactor Judge from an enum into an interface (id + systemPrompt + userPrompt(sample)) so the template ships the runner and the product supplies its own judges + sample type. Faithfulness/InjectionResistance judges are reusable for ANY LLM feature though — worth shipping as optional defaults.

portableItems.name: Langfuse Env config block + langfuseEnabled gate
portableItems.whatItIs: Env.Ai gained langfuseHost (defaults to EU cloud), langfusePublicKey?, langfuseSecretKey?, langfuseEnvironment (development|production), and a derived langfuseEnabled = both keys present & non-blank. The whole stack is opt-in: keyless -> LangfuseConfig is null -> no exporter installed -> graph runs byte-identically. NoType also hardened the shared Env loader: walk-up-from-CWD .env discovery (Gradle :server:run sets CWD to the subproject) and System.getenv precedence over .env (12-factor).
portableItems.notes: Port the langfuse* fields + langfuseEnabled into Template's Env.Ai (Template's Env.Ai today only has enabled + googleApiKey). The walk-up .env discovery + System.getenv precedence (Env.kt lines 9-27) is a STRICTLY GENERIC improvement over Template's plain Dotenv.configure().ignoreIfMissing() — port it independently; it fixes :server:run not seeing the root .env. LangfuseConfig data class is generic (rename package).

portableItems.name: Version-catalog OTel/Langfuse deps + .env.example + self-hosted Langfuse docker profile + EnvLoader + seed Gradle task
portableItems.whatItIs: Build/infra plumbing: libs.versions.toml adds koog-agents-otel (agents-features-opentelemetry, stable preview7 line) and otel-exporter-otlp 1.61.0 (the Java OTLP/HTTP exporter — the KMP one never completes the send) in a koog-cleanup bundle; .env.example documents LANGFUSE_* with a precedence gotcha; docker-compose has an opt-in `langfuse` profile (web:3000 + self-contained postgres/clickhouse/redis/minio) with a devUpLangfuse Gradle task; buildSrc/EnvLoader.kt makes .env authoritative for spawned JavaExec JVMs; a seedLangfusePrompts JavaExec task pushes constants as v1/production, gated on keys.
portableItems.notes: EnvLoader.kt is fully generic (fixes ambient-shell vars shadowing project .env in Gradle tasks) — port verbatim. The otel deps + koog-cleanup bundle are generic but tied to koog 1.0.0-preview7 — Template is on koog 0.6.2, so a Koog bump is a PREREQUISITE before any of the tracing code compiles. The docker `langfuse` profile + devUpLangfuse + docs/langfuse-self-hosting.md are generic infra worth shipping. seedLangfusePrompts task is generic mechanism but seeds the product's ConstantPromptCatalog — keep the task, parameterize the catalog.
