productSpecificExclusions.name: Cleanup / translate system prompt CONTENT + ConstantPromptCatalog
productSpecificExclusions.why: OPENAI_CLEANUP_SYSTEM_PROMPT / OPENAI_TRANSLATE_SYSTEM_PROMPT and ConstantPromptCatalog (cleanup+translate specs) are NoType's dictation-domain prompt text — the corpus-gated safety floor for voice cleanup. The template should ship the PromptProvider/catalog MECHANISM but an EMPTY or example catalog, not these prompts. Files: CleanupSystemPrompts.kt, TranslateSystemPrompts.kt, ConstantPromptCatalog.kt, PromptIdentity.kt (cleanup/translate identities).

productSpecificExclusions.name: Structured-output envelope unwrapping ({cleaned}/{translated})
productSpecificExclusions.why: unwrapStructured() and promptIdentityForCompletion() in the span adapter + PromptIdentity hardcode the JSON keys 'cleaned' and 'translated' that NoType's post-process nodes emit. Any other product has different (or no) structured envelope. Generalize to a pluggable finalizer when porting the adapter.

productSpecificExclusions.name: The four domain judges + EvalSample shape
productSpecificExclusions.why: Judge.Faithfulness/InjectionResistance/NoTranslationLeak/FormattingQuality system prompts and EvalSample(raw, cleaned, finalText, translated, sourceLang, targetLang) are dictation-cleanup/translation specific. The judge RUNNER and gate are generic; the concrete judges + sample type are product (Faithfulness + InjectionResistance are reusable enough to offer as optional defaults).

productSpecificExclusions.name: PostProcessProvider graph + faithfulness/translate gates + tone
productSpecificExclusions.why: PostProcessProvider's AIAgent graph (cleanup -> tone -> translate), HallucinationGuard text-subset check, TranslateGate cross-lingual gate, ToneRegistry, ToneGate, and the cleanup/translate-shaped trace metadata (tone, sourceLang/targetLang) are NoType's voice-dictation pipeline. Only the OTel-SDK-and-adapter wiring INSIDE it (buildProcessLifetimeSdk, NonClosingOpenTelemetry, install(OpenTelemetry) block) is generic and should be lifted out into server:core:observability so the product provider just calls it.

productSpecificExclusions.name: ZDR account boot flags + dictation minute caps
productSpecificExclusions.why: Env.Ai's openaiZdrAccount/anthropicZdrAccount/cleanupEndpointZdrAccount boot guards and free/pro minute caps are NoType's privacy-commitment + monetization domain, unrelated to observability. Do not bundle them with the Langfuse Env fields when porting.

risks: Koog version is a hard prerequisite: Template is on koog 0.6.2; the entire tracing stack is written against koog 1.0.0-preview7 (agents-features-opentelemetry, GenAIAttributes, SpanAdapter, install(OpenTelemetry) API). None of the adapter/SDK code compiles on 0.6.2. Bump Koog FIRST (and note the two non-binary-compatible Koog streams: koog 1.0.0-preview7 core must not be mixed with koog-ext 1.0.0-beta-preview7).

Koog OTel API is preview/unstable and the split between stable (agents-features-opentelemetry) and beta lines moves between releases; the SpanAdapter is a self-contained reimplementation precisely because Koog's LangfuseSpanAdapter is `internal`. A future Koog bump can break it — pin versions and rely on the koog-langfuse-tracing skill's Version notes.

Two verified self-hosted-Langfuse-v3 gotchas are load-bearing and easy to regress: (1) trace.output must be set on the INFERENCE span, not only the root CREATE_AGENT span (v3 doesn't reliably ingest agent-span observations); (2) promptVersion must be sent as freeform langfuse.observation.metadata, NOT the reserved langfuse.prompt.version int field, or the strict ingestion worker rejects the WHOLE observation. Keep these comments when porting.

The process-lifetime-SDK + NonClosingOpenTelemetry + simpleSpanProcessor + setShutdownOnAgentClose(true) combination is a single interlocking fix for the flat-trace + meter-provider-leak problem; porting it piecemeal (e.g. dropping the NonClosing wrapper, or switching to a batch processor) reintroduces either the flat trace or a per-call leak. Port as one unit.

Module placement: prefer a NEW server:core:observability module over server:core:config. server:core:config should stay dependency-light (only gains the Env fields + LangfuseConfig value type); the observability module needs koog-agents-otel + the Java OTLP exporter + ktor-client and should depend on server:core:config, not the reverse. The product's server:ai:cleanup then depends on server:core:observability.

Both global skills (koog-langfuse-tracing, langfuse-prompt-management) already encode the KNOW-HOW and ship a generified adapter reference — when porting, reconcile the template code with those skill references so they stay the single source of truth; don't fork a third copy of the adapter.

BOUNDED-timeouts rule: the Langfuse REST HttpClient and OTLP exporter must always be configured with explicit connect/request timeouts (NoType uses 3s connect / 10-15s request). These are background/eval calls but the default Ktor/OkHttp timeouts can be unbounded — easy to forget when wiring DI in a new project.

summary: This entire area is net-new in NoType — Template has NO core/audio, NO app/dictation, NO permission/keychain/native infra; its only Apple-side code is auth OAuth deep links and an image picker. NoType built a substantial, mostly-generic body of Apple-platform and permission infrastructure that future template-derived apps will need the moment they touch the mic, camera, location, contacts, secure tokens, or ship a packaged/notarized macOS desktop bundle. The genuinely reusable pieces are: (1) an expect/actual permission-gate pattern (prompting gate + read-only non-prompting status probe) with a typed Either<AppError> result, (2) a four-platform secure-credential-storage abstraction (macOS Keychain via /usr/bin/security CLI, Apple Security.framework cinterop for iOS/macOS-native, Windows DPAPI, Android Keystore, guarded raw-file dev fallback) behind a tiny KeychainBackend/DpapiBackend interface, (3) a macOS Objective-C/CoreFoundation JNA bridge (isMacOsHost gate, AppKit/ApplicationServices bindings, AVFoundation TCC request shim, AX trust + "open System Settings" deep-link), (4) reduced-motion accessibility expect/actual, and (5) the macOS desktop bundle packaging story — hardened-runtime entitlements.plist, NSMicrophoneUsageDescription/Info.plist injection, jlink module narrowing, a stable-resign dev-bundle script for TCC persistence, and Gradle plist-lint check tasks wired into `check`. The hard-won knowledge captured in KDocs (TCC silent-zero, hardened-runtime entitlements, cdhash-anchored grants resetting on rebuild, dev-launch identity attributing to the parent IDE) is itself a major portable asset. Product-specific exclusions are the audio-queue capture, the AES audio-key minting semantics, and the microphone-named (vs generic) gate/probe.

portableItems.name: macOS Objective-C / CoreFoundation JNA bridge (isMacOsHost gate + AppKit/ApplicationServices/AVFoundation bindings)
portableItems.whatItIs: The foundational `internal val isMacOsHost = System.getProperty("os.name") == "Mac OS X"` host gate plus a clean, documented JNA layer over the Objective-C runtime (objc_getClass/sel_registerName/objc_msgSend), CoreFoundation constant resolution (getGlobalVariableAddress + getPointer(0) for kCFBooleanTrue, kCFRunLoopCommonModes, kAXTrustedCheckOptionPrompt), AppKit (NSWorkspace), and ApplicationServices (AX trust, CFString). Every helper gates on isMacOsHost and never throws across the native boundary. This is the bedrock every other macOS-native JVM feature builds on and the single highest-leverage thing to port — any future template app doing desktop-native macOS work needs exactly this scaffolding.
portableItems.notes: isMacOsHost, the ObjcBridge idiom, the CoreFoundation-constant resolution idiom, and the AppKitLib/ApplicationServicesLib bindings are 100% generic. MacOsNative.kt currently mixes generic primitives with hotkey/CGEventTap constants (MacOsConstants) that are product-flavoured — split it: put isMacOsHost + the JNA Library interfaces + CF-constant lazies into a generic `core:platform-macos` (or `core:native`) module, leave the CGEventTap/hotkey constants in the product. Rename package com.m2f.template.platform. Add libs.jna + libs.jna.platform to the module. The AVFoundation TCC request shim (MacOsMicrophonePermission) generalizes to any media type by swapping AVMediaTypeAudio for AVMediaTypeVideo (camera) — keep it generic by parameterizing the media-type symbol name.

portableItems.name: Permission-gate + read-only status-probe expect/actual pattern
portableItems.whatItIs: A two-seam permission abstraction: a *prompting* gate `expect class XxxPermissionGate { suspend fun ensureGranted(): Either<AppError, Unit> }` that returns a typed Left on denial, and a *non-prompting* read-only status probe `expect class XxxPermissionStatus { fun isGranted(): Boolean? }` (true/false/null = granted/denied/unknown) safe to call on every screen entry without mutating TCC state. Per-platform actuals: JVM/macOS delegates to the AVFoundation shim, Android/iOS are documented granted/null no-ops until wired. The split between 'prompt once at point-of-use' and 'reflect status honestly in UI' is a genuinely good generic permission-UX pattern, plus the pure status-mapping helper (micAuthStatusToGranted) kept separate for unit-testability.
portableItems.notes: The PATTERN is generic but the concrete classes are microphone-named. Recommendation: create a generic `core:permissions` module exposing a `Permission` enum (Microphone, Camera, Accessibility, Notifications, Location...) and a single `expect class PermissionController { suspend fun request(p: Permission): Either<AppError, Unit>; fun status(p: Permission): PermissionStatus }` where PermissionStatus is sealed (Granted/Denied/Restricted/NotDetermined/Unknown) instead of a raw Boolean?. The JVM actual dispatches by Permission to AVFoundation (mic/camera) or AX (accessibility). Requires AppError.Audio.PermissionDenied to be generalized to AppError.Permission.Denied(permission) in core:models. Keep the prompt-once-vs-status-probe split — it is the valuable part.

portableItems.name: Four-platform secure-credential-storage abstraction (Keychain / DPAPI / Android Keystore / Security.framework cinterop / guarded dev fallback)
portableItems.whatItIs: A pluggable secret-storage facade: commonMain `expect class KeyVault { suspend fun loadOrCreate...() }` with (a) JVM three-way OS dispatch — macOS via /usr/bin/security CLI (MacSecurityCliKeychainBackend, with hardened CR-01 fixes: hex secret delivered via mode-0600 temp file on stdin not argv, stderr captured, errSecItemNotFound exit-44 distinguished from transient failures so a stored secret is never silently rotated), Windows via DPAPI CryptProtectData/CryptUnprotectData (WindowsDpapiBackend), and a system-property-guarded raw-file fallback that THROWS by default in release builds; (b) Apple via Security.framework cinterop (SecItemAdd/SecItemCopyMatching with the canonical CFDictionaryCreateMutable+CFDictionaryAddValue idiom and kSecAttrAccessibleAfterFirstUnlockThisDeviceOnly backup-hardening); (c) Android via AndroidKeyStore. The KeychainBackend/DpapiBackend interfaces are injectable for testing (InMemoryKeychainBackend). This is exactly the secure secret-at-rest abstraction every app needs (auth tokens, refresh tokens, API keys) and is dramatically better than Template's plaintext TokenStorage (multiplatform-settings).
portableItems.notes: These files live in core:audio and the public API is audio-key-shaped (loadOrCreateAudioKey, service 'com.m2f.notype.audio', account 'aes256'). Extract the backend layer into a new generic `core:securestorage` module with a `SecretStore { suspend fun get(service,account): ByteArray?; suspend fun put(service,account,bytes) }` API parameterized by service/account (the KeychainBackend interface ALREADY takes service+account precisely so it can host multiple secrets — the author anticipated this). Leave the AES key-minting/rotation semantics in the product. The Apple cinterop requires kotlinx-cinterop wiring on the module (note core:audio's appleMain currently has NO cinterop — KeyVault.apple uses the platform.Security klib that ships with K/N, which is fine). Port the security hardening verbatim (stdin temp-file, exit-44 handling, kSecAttrAccessibleAfterFirstUnlockThisDeviceOnly) — those are the load-bearing fixes. Add libs.jna.platform for the DPAPI path.

portableItems.name: macOS desktop bundle packaging: hardened-runtime entitlements + Info.plist usage-description injection + jlink module narrowing
portableItems.whatItIs: The complete Compose-Desktop nativeDistributions macOS story that Template entirely lacks: an entitlements.plist that re-declares Compose's three default hardened-runtime JVM keys (allow-jit, allow-unsigned-executable-memory, disable-library-validation — these MUST be repeated because a custom entitlementsFile replaces Compose's default wholesale) plus device.audio-input; a macOS{} block setting bundleID (CFBundleIdentifier = TCC identity anchor), packageName (CFBundleName = TCC dialog display), iconFile, entitlementsFile, and infoPlist{ extraKeysRawXml } to inject NSMicrophoneUsageDescription into the generated bundle; jvmArgs for hardened-runtime --add-opens and idle-RAM heap caps; and modules.clear()+modules(...) jlink narrowing for a small dmg. The KDoc captures the entire painful TCC discovery chain (UAT rounds 1-6). This is the canonical 'how to ship a permission-using notarizable macOS Compose app' reference.
portableItems.notes: Port entitlements.plist as a template with the four keys (the three JVM keys are mandatory generic; audio-input is the example permission to swap/extend for camera/etc.). Port the macOS{} block shape (bundleID, packageName split, entitlementsFile, infoPlist extraKeysRawXml) with placeholder usage-description text. Port the jvmArgs --add-opens trio and the heap/GC caps (generic desktop polish). Port modules.clear()+modules(...) jlink list (generic; drop java.net.http if unused). Strip the mic-specific KDoc but KEEP the distilled lesson: 'custom entitlementsFile replaces Compose defaults, repeat the 3 JVM keys'. Rename packageName/bundleID to template placeholders driven by setup.sh.

portableItems.name: Gradle plist-lint verification tasks wired into the check chain
portableItems.whatItIs: Two Gradle tasks — checkIosPlist (lints the static iOS source Info.plist for required keys) and checkMacosPlist (depends on createDistributable, lints the GENERATED NoType.app/Contents/Info.plist for the usage-description key) — both wired into `tasks.named("check")`. They parse plist XML with a hardened DocumentBuilderFactory (doctype allowed but external-DTD resolution disabled via a no-op EntityResolver, so the build never makes a network request to apple.com). This is a reusable CI guardrail pattern: fail the build loudly when a required Info.plist key regresses, instead of shipping a silently-broken bundle.
portableItems.notes: The XML-parsing + hardened-DocumentBuilderFactory + no-op-EntityResolver helper is fully generic and worth extracting into a buildSrc convention (e.g. a `plistLint(file, requiredKeys: List<String>)` helper) so the two task bodies don't duplicate ~40 lines each. Currently the required-key set is hardcoded to UIBackgroundModes:audio + NSMicrophoneUsageDescription — parameterize the key list. Keep the createDistributable dependency wiring for the macOS task. Generic value: any template app that declares ANY usage-description gets a regression guard for free.

portableItems.name: macOS AX (Accessibility) permission probe + 'Open System Settings' deep-link
portableItems.whatItIs: AccessibilityPermission expect/actual: isTrusted() (silent AXIsProcessTrusted), promptAndCheck() returning Either, and openSystemSettings() that launches the documented `x-apple.systempreferences:com.apple.preference.security?Privacy_Accessibility` URL via Desktop.browse with graceful degradation to AppError.Native.Unavailable on non-macOS/headless. Re-checked on NSApplicationDidBecomeActive so a grant from the Settings sidebar is honored without restart. The 'deep-link to the exact privacy pane + degrade gracefully' pattern is reusable for any macOS permission (Accessibility, Full Disk Access, Screen Recording, Microphone pane).
portableItems.notes: Accessibility-trust itself is niche (a power-user/automation feature), but the openSystemSettings() deep-link helper is broadly useful and should be generalized to take the pane anchor (Privacy_Accessibility / Privacy_Microphone / Privacy_Camera / Privacy_ScreenCapture). Move into the generic core:permissions or core:platform-macos module. Depends on AppError.Native.* types (port those into core:models). The iOS actual is a documented Unavailable no-op — keep that shape.

portableItems.name: Reduced-motion accessibility expect/actual (WCAG prefers-reduced-motion)
portableItems.whatItIs: A tiny, fully-generic accessibility seam: `internal expect fun prefersReducedMotion(): Boolean` + a `@Composable rememberPrefersReducedMotion()` accessor, with actuals reading NSWorkspace.accessibilityDisplayShouldReduceMotion (JVM/macOS via the AppKit JNA bridge), UIAccessibilityIsReduceMotionEnabled (iOS/Apple), and a conservative false stub (Android/non-macOS). Lets any animated UI honor the OS reduce-motion setting — pure accessibility infra with zero product coupling.
portableItems.notes: Essentially copy-paste portable — this belongs in app:designsystem (it's a UI/accessibility concern, and designsystem already targets all platforms incl. iosMain). Only dependency is the AppKit JNA bridge (AppKitLib + isMacOsHost) on the JVM side, so it must be ported together with the macOS JNA bridge item. Rename package, change Android stub as desired. Add an Android actual reading Settings.Global.TRANSITION_ANIMATION_SCALE when the mobile UI lands.

portableItems.name: Stable-resign dev-bundle launch script for macOS TCC persistence
portableItems.whatItIs: scripts/run-dev-bundle.sh — builds :composeApp:createDistributable, auto-detects a codesigning identity (or honors $NOTYPE_SIGN_IDENTITY), re-signs the .app --deep --options runtime with the entitlements so the TCC designated requirement becomes CERT-anchored (stable across rebuilds) instead of cdhash-anchored (resets every rebuild), verifies the signature, then `open`s the bundle so TCC attributes grants to the real bundle id rather than the parent IDE. Solves the universal macOS-desktop-dev pain that hotDevJvm launches a naked JVM and TCC attributes permissions to IntelliJ. Includes the tccutil reset recipe in comments.
portableItems.notes: Generic for ANY permission-using macOS Compose Desktop app. Only product coupling is the NOTYPE_SIGN_IDENTITY env var name and the com.m2f.notype bundle id in the echo/tccutil hints — parameterize via a SIGN_IDENTITY env var and read the bundle id from the build. Pairs with the entitlements.plist + macOS{} bundle item — port them together. Document it in the template README under 'developing macOS permission flows'.

productSpecificExclusions.name: MacOsAudioQueueCapture / MacOsAudioToolbox (CoreAudio AudioQueue capture)
productSpecificExclusions.why: Audio-frame capture via AudioToolbox AudioQueue Services is dictation domain. It is built ON the generic JNA bridge (so the bridge is the reusable part) but the capture pipeline, AudioFrame, BUFFER_BYTES, ASBD setup, and the TargetDataLine-vs-AudioQueue workaround are product-specific to recording voice.

productSpecificExclusions.name: MicrophoneSource expect/actual + AVAudioEngine tap (core:audio)
productSpecificExclusions.why: The microphone capture seam emitting AudioFrame flows (16kHz mono Int16, AVAudioEngine installTap, AVAudioConverter) is purely the dictation product's audio ingestion. Not template infra.

productSpecificExclusions.name: AES audio-key minting/rotation semantics (loadOrCreateAudioKey, AeadCipher, EncryptedBlob, OpusEncoder)
productSpecificExclusions.why: The 32-byte AES-256 per-install audio key, GCM per-chunk nonce envelope, opus encoding and encryption-at-rest of recordings are NoType's voice-recording-storage domain. The secure-storage BACKEND layer underneath (KeychainBackend/DpapiBackend) is the reusable part; the audio-key business logic on top is not.

productSpecificExclusions.name: iOS UIBackgroundModes:audio + audio-flavoured NSMicrophoneUsageDescription strings
productSpecificExclusions.why: The background-audio entitlement and the literal 'NoType captures your voice…' usage-description copy are dictation-specific. Port the Info.plist INJECTION mechanism and the lint task, not the audio background mode or the copy.

productSpecificExclusions.name: Microphone-named gate/probe classes and AppError.Audio hierarchy
productSpecificExclusions.why: MicrophonePermissionGate / MicrophonePermissionStatus and AppError.Audio.PermissionDenied are named for the mic. The pattern is generic but these exact symbols are product-shaped — generalize to a Permission enum + AppError.Permission rather than porting the mic-named versions verbatim.

productSpecificExclusions.name: Hotkey / CGEventTap / text-injection / installed-apps native code
productSpecificExclusions.why: MacOsHotkeyTap, MacOsInjector, InstalledAppsProvider, ForegroundAppProbe, MacOsConstants (CGEventTap flags) are the dictation hotkey + paste-into-active-app surface. Out of scope for apple-permissions and product-specific, though they share the same generic isMacOsHost/JNA bridge.

risks: Ordering: the macOS JNA bridge (MacOsNative.kt: isMacOsHost + AppKitLib/ApplicationServicesLib + CF-constant lazies) is a hard prerequisite for the permission-gate JVM actual, AccessibilityPermission JVM actual, and ReducedMotion JVM actual. Extract and port the bridge FIRST into a generic core:platform-macos (or core:native) module, then the consumers.

core:models coupling: the gates/probes/deep-links return Either<AppError, _> using AppError.Native.* (Unavailable, AccessibilityDenied) and AppError.Audio.PermissionDenied. Port/define a generic AppError.Permission.* + AppError.Native.* in Template's core:models before porting the seams, otherwise they won't compile. Prefer generalizing AppError.Audio.PermissionDenied -> AppError.Permission.Denied(permission) during the port.

Don't lift core:audio/crypto wholesale — the genuinely reusable secret-storage is the KeychainBackend/DpapiBackend backend layer (already service/account-parameterized), NOT loadOrCreateAudioKey. Split the backend into core:securestorage and leave the AES/audio key logic behind, or you drag the whole dictation crypto stack into the template.

Apple Security.framework path (KeyVault.apple) uses the platform.Security klib bundled with Kotlin/Native — no custom cinterop .def needed — but note the documented test limitation: iosX64/iosSimulatorArm64 keychain tests fail with errSecNotAvailable (-25291) because K/N test binaries run outside an .app bundle. Don't gate CI on iOS-simulator keychain round-trips; macosX64/macosArm64 tests are the safe target. Carry over that gating note.

Custom entitlementsFile REPLACES Compose Desktop's built-in default wholesale — if you port entitlements.plist you MUST keep the three JVM hardened-runtime keys (allow-jit, allow-unsigned-executable-memory, disable-library-validation) or the bundled JVM won't launch. This is the easiest thing to get wrong on port.

The plist-lint checkMacosPlist task depends on createDistributable (slow, ~30-60s) and asserts on a hardcoded build/compose/.../<AppName>.app path that embeds the packageName — parameterize the app name or it silently no-ops/fails when the template's packageName differs.

Several reusable seams currently live INSIDE the hidden app:dictation:impl module (per the contract/impl/wire convention, impl is only visible to wire). Permission/accessibility/reduced-motion infra should move UP into shared core modules (core:permissions, core:platform-macos, app:designsystem) on port so other features can consume them — don't replicate the impl-hidden placement.

Notarization is only partially present: NoType has hardened-runtime entitlements + a dev re-sign script and the .agents asc-* skills (asc-notarization, asc-workflow) reference the flow, but there is no end-to-end notarize/staple Gradle task. If the template promises 'notarization already resolved', that last mile (Developer ID signing + notarytool submit + stapler) still needs to be authored — the entitlements/signing groundwork is the reusable starting point, not a complete pipeline.

summary: NoType's `.claude` tooling is a strict superset of Template's with a few meaningful, portable upgrades. The biggest generic win is a complete rewrite of the `detekt-on-edit.sh` PostToolUse hook: instead of a slow whole-build `./gradlew detekt --daemon` per edit, it runs the standalone detekt CLI on just the edited file (~1-2s), auto-fixes formatting, reports manual-fix issues, applies per-module baselines, and is non-blocking. NoType also adds 11 new skills: 6 fully-generic App Store Connect (`asc-*`) skills vendored from `rudrankriyam/asc-skills` (tracked via `skills-lock.json`), 3 launch/drive-the-app skills (`launch-android`/`launch-ios`/`launch-desktop`), and the product-specific `notype-design`. It adds a `compose-hot-reload` MCP server (`.mcp.json`), a `.claude/launch.json` of IDE run configs, and dev scripts under `scripts/`. NoType's CLAUDE.md (~49KB vs ~9KB) adds many generic sections: a "Review-Hardened Patterns" library of real-bug lessons, a "Running & UI Verification" pipeline, an exhaustive Conventions/Code-Style/Architecture reference, and 12 numbered engineering "Rules". Template's only unique skills are the `terminal-design-*` trio (NoType replaced the Terminal design system with Aurora, so those were dropped and `terminal-`→`Aurora` renames are NOT a delta to port). The `sync-design` command and the `compose-hot-reload` MCP entry round out the deltas.

portableItems.name: Fast per-edit detekt hook (detekt-on-edit.sh rewrite)
portableItems.whatItIs: PostToolUse hook that, on each Kotlin edit, runs the standalone detekt CLI on ONLY the edited file (~1-2s) instead of a whole-build `./gradlew detekt --daemon`. Two passes: (1) formatting ruleset + --auto-correct fixes whitespace/imports/commas in place; (2) non-formatting rulesets report manual-fix issues. Auto-fetches the matching detekt CLI + formatting plugin jars once into a git-ignored cache, derives both configs from build-config/detekt-config.yml so it always matches project style, walks up to apply the nearest detekt-baseline.xml, filters known false-positives (UnusedImports under context params), caps the report, and always exits 0 (non-blocking). Massive DX upgrade over Template's version, which runs the full Gradle build on every save and can only lint server modules.
portableItems.notes: Replace Template's .claude/hooks/detekt-on-edit.sh wholesale. Pin DETEKT_VERSION to the template's detekt version (NoType uses 1.23.8). settings.json must invoke it as `bash .claude/hooks/detekt-on-edit.sh` (Template currently uses `node` — that is a latent bug, see below). Confirm the cache dir .claude/hooks/.detekt is git-ignored. No package-name coupling.

portableItems.name: settings.json detekt hook command fix (node -> bash)
portableItems.whatItIs: Template's .claude/settings.json invokes the detekt hook with `node .claude/hooks/detekt-on-edit.sh`, but the file is a bash script with a #!/usr/bin/env bash shebang — running it through node is wrong. NoType corrects this to `bash .claude/hooks/detekt-on-edit.sh`. This is the ONLY difference between the two settings.json files.
portableItems.notes: One-line fix in Template's .claude/settings.json. Ship together with the hook rewrite above.

portableItems.name: App Store Connect skill pack (asc-*)
portableItems.whatItIs: Six fully-generic skills for shipping iOS/macOS apps via the `asc` CLI: asc-cli-usage (flags/output/auth/pagination conventions), asc-id-resolver (name->ID lookups for apps/builds/versions/groups/testers), asc-build-lifecycle (find/track/expire builds, TestFlight + App Store publish), asc-submission-health (asc validate readiness, encryption declarations, review monitoring), asc-notarization (xcodebuild archive/export + Apple notarization + Developer ID trust-chain repair), and asc-workflow (repo-local .asc/workflow.json lane automation). Zero NoType-specific content — every command uses placeholder APP_ID/BUILD_ID. Any template targeting iOS/macOS distribution benefits.
portableItems.notes: Vendored from github rudrankriyam/asc-skills and tracked in skills-lock.json. Copy the six skill dirs AND port skills-lock.json so they can be re-synced/updated. Also present (duplicated) under NoType .agents/skills/asc-* — port only the .claude/skills copies. No code changes needed.

portableItems.name: skills-lock.json (vendored-skill provenance/locking)
portableItems.whatItIs: A lockfile recording the source repo (rudrankriyam/asc-skills), sourceType (github), skillPath, and a computedHash for each externally-vendored skill. Lets you track upstream provenance and re-sync/verify third-party skills rather than copy-pasting them opaquely. A generic convention worth adopting whenever the template pulls in community skills.
portableItems.notes: Port alongside the asc-* skills. Empty/extend it as the template vendors more third-party skills.

portableItems.name: launch-android skill
portableItems.whatItIs: Drive a running Android app on emulator/device for functional testing. Prefers Google's April-2026 `android` CLI (agent-first: annotated screenshots, JSON layout, label->coordinate `screen resolve`, `layout --diff`) with full `adb` fallbacks. Covers attach-to-running default flow plus a headless build/install/launch appendix, login/OAuth-deeplink/locale/dark-mode recipes, and emphasises testTag/Compose-semantics as a first-class testing concern. The methodology is generic; only app identity is NoType-specific.
portableItems.notes: Replace package `com.m2f.notype`, launcher `.MainActivity`, and deep link `notype://auth/callback` with template equivalents (or {{PLACEHOLDER}} tokens wired into setup-project). Drop the OAuth-deeplink recipe if the template lacks OAuth. Everything else is reusable verbatim.

portableItems.name: launch-ios skill
portableItems.whatItIs: Drive the running iOS Simulator app for functional testing via `xcrun simctl` (state: screenshots, openurl deep links, push, privacy pre-grants, status_bar override, log stream) + computer-use (taps/typing). Includes a headless `xcodebuild -target iosApp` build/install/launch appendix that needs NO shared Xcode scheme, plus OAuth/permission/push/locale/App-Group-IPC recipes. Generic KMP-iosApp methodology; app identity and the App-Group IPC recipe are NoType-specific.
portableItems.notes: Replace bundle id com.m2f.notype, product name `notype`, and iosApp project/target names. Drop the App-Group-IPC and keyboard-extension-memory-limit recipes (NoType-specific). The simctl + xcodebuild -target pipeline is the valuable generic core.

portableItems.name: launch-desktop skill
portableItems.whatItIs: Canonical JVM Desktop run/verify pipeline using Compose Hot Reload + the compose-hot-reload MCP. Attach-vs-autonomous-run modes, the MCP drive loop (status -> get_semantic_tree -> click/type_text/scroll -> take_screenshot), notes on slow first cold config-cache connect, and edit->reload->re-screenshot iteration without relaunch. The CHR+MCP methodology is fully generic for any Compose Desktop template; it carries the heaviest NoType coupling (one-instance CGEvent-hotkey freeze rule, floating HUD overlay, global dictation hotkey fallbacks).
portableItems.notes: Port together with the compose-hot-reload MCP server entry in .mcp.json (the `:composeApp:hotMcpServerJvm` stdio server) — the skill is useless without it. Swap main class com.m2f.notype.MainKt. STRIP the product-specific bits: the HARD one-instance/CGEvent-freeze rule, the HUD/recording-pill NSWindow fallback, and the global dictation-hotkey CGEvent fallback. Keep the generic MCP drive loop and the computer-use fallback-for-OS-dialogs note.

portableItems.name: compose-hot-reload MCP server (.mcp.json)
portableItems.whatItIs: Adds a stdio MCP server `compose-hot-reload` that runs `./gradlew --no-daemon --quiet --console=plain :composeApp:hotMcpServerJvm`, exposing the running desktop app's Compose semantic tree to the agent (status/get_semantic_tree/click/type_text/scroll/take_screenshot). This is the only delta between the two .mcp.json files and is what makes deterministic, non-pixel UI verification of the desktop app possible.
portableItems.notes: Append the `compose-hot-reload` block to Template's .mcp.json. Requires the composeApp `hotMcpServerJvm` Gradle task to exist on the template (verify the Compose Hot Reload plugin/task is wired in composeApp). Pairs with launch-desktop and the compose-screen/feature verification step.

portableItems.name: Live-verification step in compose-screen and feature skills
portableItems.whatItIs: Both shared scaffolding skills gained a final step: after generating a screen/feature, verify it live by running with Compose Hot Reload and driving it via the compose-hot-reload MCP (get_semantic_tree to assert nodes exist, take_screenshot for visual check), referencing the launch-desktop skill, with a one-instance caveat. Closes the generate->verify loop instead of stopping at code generation.
portableItems.notes: Port just the appended verification paragraph into Template's compose-screen and feature SKILL.md (drop the NoType-specific 'one instance only / CGEvent' caveat, keep the MCP loop). Do NOT port the AuroraTheme/AuroraButton renames in these files — Template correctly uses TerminalTheme/TerminalButton.

portableItems.name: CLAUDE.md: Review-Hardened Patterns section
portableItems.whatItIs: A library of generic Kotlin/coroutine/KMP anti-patterns distilled from real code-review bugs: never `runCatching` around suspend Either/Result code (swallows CancellationException — use Either.catch); always withTimeout around Deferred.await for resource init; cached Deferred caches terminal failures so hold it in an AtomicReference and compareAndSet(null) on failure to allow retry; reactive guards must combine(observeA, observeB) not read prefs once in LaunchedEffect(Unit); platform-conditional UI must hide capability toggles when the per-platform Koin binding is empty; third-party downloads need connect+request timeouts, incremental SHA-256 verify, HTTPS-only. Broadly applicable to any Arrow+coroutines+KMP codebase.
portableItems.notes: Port the section but genericize the examples (drop NoType references like DeferredOnDeviceProvider, Whisper ensureAvailable, model-weight GGUF downloads). The download-on-first-use-model-swaps subsection is largely product-specific — keep only the generic 'large download has UI/timeout/prefetch obligations' principle if at all.

portableItems.name: CLAUDE.md: exhaustive Conventions / Code Style / Architecture reference
portableItems.whatItIs: Large reference blocks absent from Template: Naming Patterns (file-per-declaration, MVI file naming, no I-prefix, data object vs data class for intents, test backtick names), Code Style mirroring detekt-config.yml (120 col, trailing commas, no wildcard imports, max function 65 lines, max class 600, named args at 3+, no !!, inject dispatchers), Error Handling (Either/AppError/apiCall on client, Raise<DomainError>/conduit on server, zipOrAccumulate), Architecture component-responsibility table, Key Abstractions, Entry Points, Architectural Constraints, and Anti-Patterns. These document the template's own conventions and are generic except for package names.
portableItems.notes: High value but verbose — port selectively. Swap com.m2f.notype->com.m2f.template and fix module list (NoType has app:dashboard/documents/profile and server:groups/files/ai; Template's module set differs). The 7-API-interface and AuroraTheme references must become Template's actual counts/TerminalTheme. Risk: drift if pasted without reconciling against Template's real modules.

portableItems.name: CLAUDE.md: numbered engineering Rules 1-12
portableItems.whatItIs: A concise, fully-generic engineering rulebook: Think Before Coding, Simplicity First, Surgical Changes, Goal-Driven Execution, use the model only for judgment calls, token budgets are not advisory, surface conflicts don't average them, read before you write, tests verify intent not just behavior, checkpoint after every significant step, match codebase conventions even if you disagree, fail loud. No product or even KMP coupling — applies to any project.
portableItems.notes: Copy verbatim into Template's CLAUDE.md. Zero adaptation. Reconcile/merge with Template's existing 'Core Principles' and 'Workflow Orchestration' sections to avoid duplication.

portableItems.name: CLAUDE.md: Running & UI Verification (Compose Hot Reload + MCP) section
portableItems.whatItIs: Top-level CLAUDE.md guidance that the default desktop run/verify pipeline is Compose Hot Reload + the compose-hot-reload MCP (not plain :composeApp:run or computer-use), summarizing the run modes, the MCP drive loop, the slow-first-connect warning, and the edit->reload->re-screenshot loop. Makes the verification methodology discoverable at the CLAUDE.md level, not just in a skill.
portableItems.notes: Port alongside the compose-hot-reload MCP entry and launch-desktop skill. Strip the one-instance/CGEvent-freeze rule and the HUD/dictation-hotkey computer-use fallbacks (product-specific). Swap main class name.

portableItems.name: server-watch.sh dev script
portableItems.whatItIs: Generic dev watch-restart loop for a Ktor backend: polls source mtimes across server/shared/core (plus .env and libs.versions.toml) every 2s, and on change recompiles + restarts `:server:run`. Self-contained (no entr/fswatch dependency), with clean trap-based shutdown and port takeover. Solves the fact that Ktor's in-process auto-reload doesn't work with the SuspendApp + context-receiver module lambda pattern this template uses.
portableItems.notes: Swap APP_MAIN=com.m2f.notype.ApplicationKt to the template's server main class. Everything else (watch dirs, polling, BSD-stat signature, trap) is generic. macOS-only `stat -f` — add a Linux `stat -c` branch for CI portability.

portableItems.name: .claude/launch.json (IDE run configurations)
portableItems.whatItIs: A versioned set of IDE run configs: server-watch (wraps server-watch.sh), server (:server:run), composeApp-desktop (wraps desktop-hot.sh), composeApp-desktop-plain, composeApp-web (wasmJsBrowserDevelopmentRun --continuous), docker-services (devUp), docs-static (python http.server over docs/). A handy onboarding artifact giving every contributor the same one-click run targets. Template has none.
portableItems.notes: Port the generic configs (server, server-watch, composeApp-desktop-plain, composeApp-web, docker-services, docs-static). composeApp-desktop depends on desktop-hot.sh which carries macOS/HUD specifics — port a genericized desktop-hot.sh first or point it at :composeApp:run.

portableItems.name: Release-gate scripts (binary-size / idle-RSS caps)
portableItems.whatItIs: Two parameterizable Compose Desktop release gates: check-binary-size.sh fails the build if the packaged .dmg exceeds a MB cap (default 80), and check-idle-rss.sh launches the packaged .app, waits 60s, and fails if idle RSS exceeds a cap (default 150 MB). The size/footprint-budget-enforcement PATTERN is generic and valuable for any desktop app that cares about distribution size and memory.
portableItems.notes: macOS-only (dmg path, NoType.app, `ps -o rss=`, `stat -f%z`). Parameterize the app/product name and dmg/app paths; the default 80MB/150MB caps are NoType NFRs — make them args (already are) and document them as examples, not template defaults.

productSpecificExclusions.name: notype-design skill
productSpecificExclusions.why: Entirely product brand assets: the Aurora design system (neon cyan/violet/magenta palette, Space Grotesk/Manrope/JetBrains Mono fonts), the two non-negotiable brand signatures (animated aurora border + voice waveform), HTML/JSX UI kit, fonts, screenshots, README tone rules. Tied to NoType's voice-first identity. Template has its own neutral Terminal design system instead.

productSpecificExclusions.name: AuroraTheme/Aurora* renames across skills, agents, CLAUDE.md
productSpecificExclusions.why: NoType replaced Template's Terminal design system with Aurora, so its compose-screen, create-app-module, code-reviewer.md, and CLAUDE.md reference AuroraTheme/AuroraButton/AuroraText. Template correctly uses TerminalTheme/Terminal*; these renames must NOT be ported — they would break the template's design-system mapping.

productSpecificExclusions.name: Launch-skill product identity and HUD/dictation specifics
productSpecificExclusions.why: Within the otherwise-generic launch-android/ios/desktop skills: package/bundle com.m2f.notype, deep link notype://auth/callback, the floating HUD/recording-pill NSWindow, the global dictation hotkey (Right-Cmd CGEvent tap), the one-instance CGEvent-freeze hazard, and App-Group IPC for the privacy-mode keyboard extension are all dictation-product specific and must be stripped or tokenized when porting.

productSpecificExclusions.name: run-dev-bundle.sh and desktop-hot.sh macOS TCC / dictation specifics
productSpecificExclusions.why: desktop-hot.sh re-adds an --add-opens so the dictation HUD doesn't steal focus; run-dev-bundle.sh wraps createDistributable specifically so macOS TCC attributes Accessibility/full-disk permissions to com.m2f.notype rather than the IDE — both exist to serve the global dictation hotkey's OS-permission needs, which a generic template app won't have.

risks: Ordering: the compose-hot-reload MCP entry (.mcp.json), the launch-desktop skill, the compose-screen/feature verification step, and the CLAUDE.md 'Running & UI Verification' section are a unit — port them together or none works. The MCP requires a `:composeApp:hotMcpServerJvm` Gradle task; verify it exists in Template before porting.

detekt-on-edit.sh hardcodes DETEKT_VERSION (1.23.8) and derives configs from build-config/detekt-config.yml — confirm both match Template's detekt setup before porting, and ensure the settings.json invocation switches from `node` to `bash` in the same change (otherwise the new hook still won't run).

The asc-* skills are duplicated in NoType under both .claude/skills/ and .agents/skills/ — port only the .claude/skills copies plus skills-lock.json to avoid duplication; the .agents/skills tree appears to be a parallel runtime location, not a second source of truth.

Many CLAUDE.md reference sections embed NoType module names (app:dashboard/documents/profile, server:groups/files/ai), counts (7 API interfaces, Sdk delegation list), and AuroraTheme. Pasting them into Template without reconciling against Template's actual module graph will introduce documentation drift that future forks inherit.

Several scripts (server-watch.sh, check-binary-size.sh, check-idle-rss.sh, run-dev-bundle.sh) are macOS-only (BSD `stat -f`, `ps -o rss=`, dmg/.app paths). If the template's CI runs on Linux, add portable branches or guard them as macOS-only dev helpers.

Do NOT let the AuroraTheme<->TerminalTheme rename leak in while porting the shared-skill verification steps or code-reviewer.md — diffing shows the only non-rename delta in compose-screen/feature/create-app-module/code-reviewer is the verification paragraph (plus, in code-reviewer, just the theme-name line). Port the paragraph, discard the rename.

area: app-features (client feature modules: auth, admin, dashboard, documents, profile, privacy)
summary: Across the six shared feature modules, NoType's MVI cores barely diverged from Template: auth (Login/Register/ForgotPassword/InviteAccept ViewModels, Models, Mutations, Intents, Events), profile (Model/ViewModel/tier content), admin, documents, and the entire privacy/GDPR suite (AccountDeletion, ConsentGate, LegalDocument, PrivacySettings) are byte-identical after package normalization — the GDPR + account-deletion(token)/consent/legal flows already live in Template and need no back-port. The real generic evolutions are structural and navigation-layer: NoType added core:navigation primitives (NavSelectionSignal, navigateAdd, navigationModule) that Template lacks entirely (Template's core:navigation is just Route.kt), a reusable wired-pane pattern (PrivacyPane) that lets one screen render both as a pushed route and embedded in-shell, a persistent-nav-rail master-detail shell that swaps injected @Composable content slots in place (no route push, rail never disappears), an expect/actual cross-feature content-injection seam so a host feature can embed another feature's UI without an impl dependency (and keep the shared nav entry in commonMain despite a wasmJs classpath gap), and an InMemorySettings test fake. Everything NoType added to the dashboard's domain content (dictation status, HomeReadiness, the slot actuals) and the Aurora design reskin of every Screen is product-specific. The Screens currently all depend on Aurora* design-system components (NoType) vs Terminal* (Template) — a 1:1 component rename that is the surface area of the design-system swap, not a logic change.

portableItems.name: NavSelectionSignal + navigationModule
portableItems.whatItIs: An app-wide one-shot signal (MutableStateFlow<String?> with request/consume) that lets EXTERNAL entry points (a desktop tray item, a deep-link boot path) ask an already-mounted master-detail shell to select a specific in-shell pane, WITHOUT a route push and WITHOUT touching the back stack. Solves the real Navigation-3 gap: a data object route can't express 'swap the mounted shell's pane', and a data class variant would push a duplicate shell. String-typed so core:navigation stays feature-free; the feature provides the key constants. Registered via a Koin module included in the shared DI graph. Has a commonTest. Generic for any app with a hub-and-spoke / sidebar shell plus external (tray/notification/deep-link) entry points.
portableItems.notes: Lives in core:navigation (technically another agent's area) but is the keystone of the dashboard navigation-signal pattern. Rename package com.m2f.notype.navigation -> com.m2f.template.navigation. The KDoc references DashboardNavKey/WR-04/tray — genericize the comment (drop NoType-specific deployment notes) but keep the explanation of WHY a route push can't do this. navigationModule must be wired into sharedModule. No new deps (kotlinx-coroutines already present).

portableItems.name: navigateAdd back-stack helper
portableItems.whatItIs: A pure, Compose-free navigation primitive: append a Route to a MutableList<Route> only if it isn't already the top (idempotent-on-top), and NEVER clear the stack. Prevents duplicate-shell pushes when an external 'Open app'/'Settings' callback fires while already on the shell, and enforces a single-clear()-site invariant that avoids a session-expired race. Trivially unit-testable.
portableItems.notes: Pairs with NavSelectionSignal. Strip the NoType-specific KDoc (App.kt/BootDesktopShell/BootDictationHotkeys/WR-04 references) down to the generic contract: append-only + idempotent-on-top. Rename package. Zero deps.

portableItems.name: Reusable wired-pane pattern (PrivacyPane)
portableItems.whatItIs: Extracts the ViewModel acquisition (koinViewModel) + load-on-enter + event collection + Screen call OUT of the Navigation-3 entry block into a standalone @Composable. The same pane is then rendered in two contexts: as a pushed route (real onBack pops the stack) and embedded inside a host shell's content slot (onBack = null, so the host's persistent rail owns top-level nav). Decouples 'a screen plus its wiring' from 'a navigation destination', which is what makes a feature screen embeddable in another feature's shell. Generic convention upgrade to the wire-layer EntryProviderScope pattern.
portableItems.notes: The PrivacyPane file itself is generic minus the SetTracingConsent callback (drop that one line — it's product-specific). The pattern is the valuable thing: bake it into the create-app-module / compose-screen skills so new features emit an XxxPane.kt that the wire entry delegates to. Port alongside the nullable-onBack screen convention below.

portableItems.name: Master-detail persistent-nav-rail shell with content slots
portableItems.whatItIs: A responsive shell that keeps a persistent nav rail always visible and swaps the detail pane in place via a when(selectedNavItem) over injected @Composable content lambdas (settingsContent/libraryContent/... passed in from the wire layer). No route is pushed for top-level nav, so the rail never disappears and no screen is ever stranded without navigation. The VM is purely presentational (event type Nothing): NavItemSelected only updates selectedNavItem, asserted model-only in tests. A cleaner alternative to Template's bottom-nav/sidebar-that-navigates-away dashboard, and consistent with the user's recorded 'sidebar items replace the content panel, don't navigate to a separate screen' preference.
portableItems.notes: Port the PATTERN, not the file. NoType's DashboardScreen/Model/Intent are saturated with dictation content (status cards, HomeReadiness, Aurora branding, StatusUpdated/dictation-status) and must NOT be copied. Template's dashboard already has selectedNavItem + DashboardSidebar — the upgrade is: (1) make detail panes injected @Composable slots from the wire layer instead of route pushes, (2) keep the VM presentational with model-only nav assertions. Best delivered as guidance/skill update plus a minimal generic example, not a code copy.

portableItems.name: expect/actual cross-feature content-injection seam
portableItems.whatItIs: A platform-seam pattern (data class binding + expect @Composable producer) that lets a host feature's shell embed UI owned by ANOTHER feature without an impl-level dependency, and keeps the shared nav entry in commonMain even when one target (wasmJs) lacks the other feature's classpath: commonMain declares `expect fun xxxContent(...): @Composable () -> Unit` / `expect fun rememberXxxBinding(): Binding`, the nonWasmJs actual returns the real cross-feature pane (resolved via koinInject), and the wasmJs actual returns an inert default. This is the generic mechanism that makes the slot-based shell above possible while respecting module visibility and per-target classpaths.
portableItems.notes: The MECHANISM is generic and worth documenting in CLAUDE.md / the create-app-module skill as the sanctioned way to embed one feature inside another's shell. The specific seams (DashboardStatusBinding fields, dictation Settings pane) are product-specific — do not copy the bindings. Also note the contract-level nav-key constants object (DashboardNavKey) as the single-source-of-truth pattern shared between impl model and external callers; keep HOME/SETTINGS, drop dictation-specific keys.

portableItems.name: InMemorySettings test fake
portableItems.whatItIs: A hand-rolled in-memory implementation of com.russhwolf.settings.Settings for commonTest, used to back PreferencesStorage in ViewModel tests without the unavailable MapSettings / multiplatform-settings-test artifact. Generic for ANY ViewModel/repository test that depends on PreferencesStorage or Settings.
portableItems.notes: Currently duplicated as a per-module internal test class. Promote to core:testing (e.g. core/testing/.../InMemorySettings.kt) so every feature reuses it instead of re-implementing. Rename package; no product coupling at all.

portableItems.name: Nullable onBack dual-context screen convention
portableItems.whatItIs: Screen signature uses `onBack: (() -> Unit)? = null` and conditionally renders the Back button only when non-null, so the SAME callbacks-only Screen works both as a standalone pushed route (real Back) and embedded in a shell where a persistent rail owns navigation (Back hidden). Small but reusable convention that unlocks screen reuse across navigation contexts.
portableItems.notes: Adopt as a default in the compose-screen skill for any screen that might be embedded. The PrivacySettingsScreen delta also adds a product-specific tracing-consent section — port only the onBack nullability change, not the tracing UI.

productSpecificExclusions.name: DashboardModel / DashboardIntent dictation status
productSpecificExclusions.why: NoType stripped Template's generic dashboard fields (metrics/processes/activities/deployment, profile/admin) and replaced them with dictation-only state: micPermissionGranted, accessibilityTrusted, chordDisplay, HomeActivationMode mode, signedIn, plus StatusUpdated/NavItemSelected intents tied to dictation. Keep Template's generic dashboard content; do not adopt NoType's model.

productSpecificExclusions.name: HomeReadiness.isReadyToDictate
productSpecificExclusions.why: Pure function gating the Home hero on signed-in + mic-granted + macOS-Accessibility-trusted — entirely a dictation prerequisite check.

productSpecificExclusions.name: Dashboard slot actuals (Settings/Library/Dictionary/Privacy/Status nonWasmJs)
productSpecificExclusions.why: The actual implementations resolve the dictation HotkeySettingsViewModel and inject dictation panes (HotkeySettingsPane). The slot MECHANISM is generic; these specific actuals are product content.

productSpecificExclusions.name: PreferencesStorage dictation prefs
productSpecificExclusions.why: Almost all new prefs (hotkeyMode/binding, translateHotkey*, sttMode, cleanupMode, tracingConsent, hudSize, appToneMap, preferredTerms) are dictation/transcription/HUD/tone domain. The StateFlow-backed reactive-pref pattern is generic, but the fields are product-specific (and core:storage is another agent's area).

productSpecificExclusions.name: Aurora design reskin of all Screens
productSpecificExclusions.why: Every feature Screen swapped Terminal* design components for Aurora* (AuroraText/AuroraButton/AuroraCard/AuroraTheme...) and rebranded copy (Login ASCII-art terminal panel -> Aurora glass cards/waveform). This is the design-system swap surface, a 1:1 component rename with no logic change — not a portable feature improvement. LocalTitleBarInset is a NoType-only desktop borderless-window concern.

productSpecificExclusions.name: tracingConsent in PrivacySettings
productSpecificExclusions.why: Local-only Langfuse/transcript-tracing consent toggle (SetTracingConsent intent/mutation/model field + screen section + VM PreferencesStorage write). Specific to NoType's transcription-tracing pipeline; the GDPR consent flows around it are already generic and shared.

productSpecificExclusions.name: Product StringKeyResolver entries
productSpecificExclusions.why: Auth StringKeyResolver added ACCESSIBILITY_DENIED_*, SIGN_IN_BANNER_*, AUDIO_PERMISSION_DENIED, AUDIO_DEVICE_UNAVAILABLE, INJECTION_CLIPBOARD_FALLBACK_USED, TRAY_MOUNT_FAILED_NOTICE — all dictation/desktop-tray/mic affordances.

risks: Ordering: port the core:navigation primitives (NavSelectionSignal, navigateAdd, navigationModule) FIRST and wire navigationModule into sharedModule; the reusable-pane and shell-slot patterns and any external-entry wiring depend on them.

core:navigation and core:storage are nominally other agents' focus areas — NavSelectionSignal/navigateAdd/InMemorySettings/PreferencesStorage findings overlap; coordinate so the navigation primitives land once and aren't double-ported.

Do NOT copy NoType's DashboardScreen/Model/Intent wholesale — they are dictation-saturated and would drag product code into Template. Port the master-detail-slot PATTERN against Template's existing generic dashboard instead.

The slot mechanism relies on expect/actual across source sets (commonMain entry + nonWasmJs actual + wasmJs inert actual). If Template's target set differs (e.g. no wasmJs, or different sharing), the seam must be re-pointed to the right intermediate source set or it won't compile.

All Screens are coupled to Aurora* components in NoType and Terminal* in Template; when porting any Screen-level change (e.g. nullable onBack), apply it against Template's Terminal* imports — don't drag Aurora names across.
InMemorySettings should be promoted to core:testing rather than copied per-module to avoid the duplication NoType already has.
NavSelectionSignal is string-typed by design to avoid a core:navigation -> feature dependency; preserve that — do not 'improve' it into a typed enum that reintroduces the cycle.

The 'password -> token' rename in account-deletion/invite flows is already consistent across BOTH repos (AccountDeletionViewModelTest and AuthRoutes identical), so it is not a delta to port — avoid re-applying it.

area: app-shell-docs (composeApp App.kt/AppNavHost/main entry points/DI bootstrap, shared aggregator, iosApp shell, README/docs/CONTRIBUTING, setup.sh, setup-project skill)
summary: NoType's app shell diverged from Template in two distinct ways. (1) A set of GENERIC desktop-shell capabilities that any JVM-desktop app benefits from: a real macOS title-bar inset CompositionLocal (LocalTitleBarInset) with edge-to-edge transparent-title-bar window chrome, a native macOS dark-mode detector (rememberSystemDarkTheme expect/actual that polls AppleInterfaceStyle, working around compose-jb#1986), a single-instance file-lock guard, a Kermit file-log writer for packaged-app observability, window minimize-to-tray lifecycle with pure unit-testable decision helpers (shouldHideOnClose / shouldRegisterPreferencesHandler), reusable SystemTray + floating-NSPanel + JFrame Swing bootstraps, pure HUD/window placement math, an append-only navigation helper (navigateAdd) and an external-nav-pane signal (NavSelectionSignal) now living in core:navigation with a navigationModule, and a parameterized window-size/min-size + JLink module-narrowing + heap-capping packaging recipe. (2) A heavily generalized setup.sh whitelabel wizard (helper functions, OAuth-scheme/Compose-generated-resource-package rename, idempotent move guard, broadened verification, build excludes) that is fully generic. The DI bootstrap was generalized from a flat allAppModules list to an expect/actual seam, and sharedModule likewise became expect/actual. Most docs/README/iosApp/MainViewController/StringKeyResolver deltas are pure whitelabel rename output (the product result, not portable). Product-specific exclusions are the dictation HUD content, tray recording animation, cap-note semantics, global hotkey bridge, and mic/AX/plist build wiring — though the underlying mechanisms (floating overlay window, global hotkey boot seam, tray icon, plist-lint Gradle task) are reusable scaffolds.

portableItems.name: LocalTitleBarInset CompositionLocal + edge-to-edge transparent macOS title bar
portableItems.whatItIs: A staticCompositionLocalOf<Dp> (default 0.dp) that lets foreground content offset below macOS traffic lights while the themed background flows edge-to-edge behind a transparent full-window-content title bar. The JVM main.kt sets apple.awt.* client props (fullWindowContent / transparentTitleBar / windowTitleVisible=false), a dark window background, and provides the real inset (28.dp) only on macOS via CompositionLocalProvider. Any KMP+Compose desktop app with a non-white theme wants this — the default white macOS title bar clashes with any dark/branded UI.
portableItems.notes: LocalTitleBarInset belongs in app:designsystem (Template has the equivalent TerminalTheme module). Move the inset definition there; in Template it can default 0.dp and the JVM main provides it. The only product coupling is the 0x07080C background color and 'NoType' title — parameterize the background to TerminalTheme.colors.bg and the title to rootProject. DashboardScreen's consumption of the inset is feature-specific; the primitive itself is generic.

portableItems.name: rememberSystemDarkTheme() expect/actual (native macOS dark-mode detection)
portableItems.whatItIs: A @Composable expect fun returning whether the OS is in dark mode. The JVM actual reads `defaults read -g AppleInterfaceStyle` and polls every 2s so a live System Settings toggle is reflected without relaunch — working around compose-jb#1986 where isSystemInDarkTheme() does not track macOS appearance on desktop. Android/iOS actuals just delegate to isSystemInDarkTheme(). Process I/O is runCatching-wrapped and fails to light.
portableItems.notes: Rename package only. CAVEAT: NoType dropped the wasmJs target from composeApp, so there is no wasmJs actual — Template still ships wasmJs, so the port MUST add a wasmJs actual (delegate to isSystemInDarkTheme()) or commonMain won't compile. Wire it into the root theme call in App.kt (Template passes nothing to TerminalTheme today).

portableItems.name: SingleInstanceGuard (OS file-lock single-instance guard)
portableItems.whatItIs: A JVM object that holds an exclusive FileChannel.tryLock() in ~/Library/Application Support/<App>/single-instance.lock for the process lifetime; OS releases on exit/crash so there's no stale-pidfile problem. acquire() returns false if another instance holds it. Fails OPEN on any error (a guard must never block launch). Generic for any desktop app that must be a singleton (global hotkeys, tray, daemons).
portableItems.notes: Parameterize the lock-dir app-name (currently 'NoType' and the macOS 'Library/Application Support' path). Make the directory resolution cross-platform (Windows %APPDATA%, Linux ~/.local/share) for a clean template. Comes with a passing test that uses the OverlappingFileLockException in-process path.

portableItems.name: FileLogWriter + installFileLogging() (packaged-app file logging)
portableItems.whatItIs: A Kermit LogWriter that appends to ~/Library/Logs/<App>/<app>-client.log with bounded growth (truncate at 5MB at construction + every 500 writes), thread-safe synchronized append, and a constructor that never throws (degrades to no-op). installFileLogging() installs it alongside platformLogWriter as the first line of main(), guarded by an AtomicBoolean so a later setLogWriters can't clobber it. Solves the real problem that a JVM app launched via macOS `open` writes no stdout anywhere observable.
portableItems.notes: Parameterize the app name / log path (cross-platform log dir). No product coupling beyond the literal paths. Pairs naturally with the existing Kermit usage in Template. Useful for every packaged desktop build.

portableItems.name: navigateAdd() append-only nav helper + NavSelectionSignal + navigationModule (in core:navigation)
portableItems.whatItIs: navigateAdd(stack, route) is a pure, Compose-free, idempotent-on-top back-stack append (no-op if route already on top, never clears). NavSelectionSignal is a tiny StateFlow<String?> request/consume primitive for asking an already-mounted shell to switch panes from an EXTERNAL entry point (tray, hotkey) where a route push can't express 'swap pane'. Both are wired via a new navigationModule Koin module. These generalize the back-stack discipline that App.kt already practices (single clear() writer invariant).
portableItems.notes: navigateAdd and the navigationModule are fully generic — port as-is (rename package). NavSelectionSignal is generic but only earns its keep once an app has an external entry point (tray/hotkey/deeplink) that targets a master-detail shell; include it but note the dashboard observes requestedNavKey. Template's sharedModule is NOT expect/actual today, so to add navigationModule cleanly either include it in commonMain sharedModule or adopt the expect/actual sharedModule pattern (below).

portableItems.name: expect/actual allAppModules + expect/actual sharedModule DI seam
portableItems.whatItIs: NoType turned the flat `allAppModules = listOf(sharedModule, appModule)` into an `expect val allAppModules: List<Module>` with per-target actuals, and likewise made `sharedModule` an expect/actual. This lets a target (e.g. wasmJs) omit modules it can't satisfy (no SQLite driver, no native feature) without polluting commonMain — a clean, generic pattern for KMP DI graphs that differ per platform.
portableItems.notes: The PATTERN is generic and worth porting (it's how KMP apps cleanly handle per-platform DI). But NoType's actuals pull product modules (aiModule, dictationModule, cleanupOnDeviceModule, platformDatabaseModule). Port only the seam: commonMain `expect val`, a nonWasmJs actual = [sharedModule, appModule, navigationModule(...), platformDatabaseModule], and a wasmJs actual = [sharedModule, appModule]. Requires Template to introduce a nonWasmJsMain source set + applyDefaultHierarchyTemplate grouping (see build.gradle item).

portableItems.name: Generalized setup.sh whitelabel wizard
portableItems.whatItIs: A substantial rewrite of the fork-rename script: extracted a reusable sed_in_matched_files() helper, a move_package_dir() guard that returns early when src==dest (prevents an rm -rf wiping the tree when names are unchanged — a real footgun in the old version), new rename coverage for the OAuth deep-link scheme (AndroidManifest android:scheme, .env, server Env.kt defaults, kotlin `scheme://auth/callback` literals), the Compose-generated resource package (rootProject.name with separators stripped -> `<ident>.<module>.generated.resources` imports and packageOfResClass overrides), JVM window title, WASM <title>, devcontainer name, broader verification grep (yml/md/json/xcconfig + graphify-out exclusion), a residual bare-'template' literal scanner, and a build verification that excludes the known :server:distZip/distTar duplicate-jar failure and tails the log on failure.
portableItems.notes: This is the single highest-value doc/scaffolding port — every future fork benefits and several changes are bug fixes (the move_package_dir src==dest guard; the broadened OAuth-scheme + generated-resource-package rename that Template's setup.sh misses entirely). Port wholesale, but reset OLD_* constants back to template defaults (OLD_PROJECT_NAME_LOWER=template, OLD_OAUTH_SCHEME=template, OLD_DB_NAME=application) and drop the NoType-specific :server:distZip/distTar exclude only if Template doesn't have that failure (verify first — it likely does, it's described as a template-level Gradle issue).

portableItems.name: Window size/min-size pinning + JLink module narrowing + heap caps (Compose Desktop packaging recipe)
portableItems.whatItIs: main.kt opens at DEFAULT_WINDOW_SIZE and pins window.minimumSize so the window can only grow from a known-good layout (prevents responsive-layout clipping below the breakpoint). build.gradle.kts narrows the bundled JDK via modules.clear()+explicit module list (java.base/desktop/naming/sql/management/jdk.unsupported) to shrink the dmg, and caps heap/GC (-Xmx256m -Xms64m -XX:+UseSerialGC -XX:MaxRAMPercentage=50) for a low idle-RAM desktop app. Plus per-OS iconFile wiring (macos.icns/windows.ico/linux.png) and packageName-vs-bundleID split documentation.
portableItems.notes: The min-window-size pattern, JLink module list, heap caps, and per-OS iconFile wiring are generic Compose Desktop hygiene Template lacks (Template's main.kt is a 12-line `Window { App() }` and its build has no nativeDistributions tuning). Port the structure; the specific numbers (1120x760, 256m) are app-tunable defaults. EXCLUDE the macOS mic entitlement/NSMicrophoneUsageDescription/--add-opens jvmArgs — those are dictation-specific.

portableItems.name: Pure unit-testable shell decision helpers (shouldHideOnClose / shouldRegisterPreferencesHandler)
portableItems.whatItIs: Two Compose-free, AWT-free pure functions that isolate desktop-shell decisions (hide-to-tray-on-close gated on tray-mount success; register the macOS Cmd+, preferences handler only when APP_PREFERENCES is supported) so the native/EDT-bound side stays a thin wrapper and the logic is unit-tested (WindowCloseDecisionTest, TrayNavigateTest). A clean, copyable pattern for testing otherwise-untestable AWT/native glue.
portableItems.notes: shouldHideOnClose is generic for any minimize-to-tray desktop app. shouldRegisterPreferencesHandler is generic for any macOS app wanting a native Settings menu item. Both are one-liners + tests; the value is the PATTERN (extract pure decision from native glue) more than the code. PreferencesHandlerGate lives in the dictation package — move to a neutral package on port.

portableItems.name: TrayIconBootstrap + HudWindowBootstrap + CapNoteWindow Swing surface MECHANISMS
portableItems.whatItIs: Reusable JVM Swing scaffolds: TrayIconBootstrap owns a java.awt.SystemTray icon with a popup menu, animated frames, a status-dot overlay, and AWTException-safe mount() returning a Boolean (headless-CI safe). HudWindowBootstrap creates a transparent undecorated always-on-top non-focusable ComposeWindow promoted to a non-activating floating NSPanel, with pure computeHudOrigin() placement math (multi-monitor aware, clamped). CapNoteWindow wraps a Compose UI in a JFrame via ComposePanel. The product CONTENT is specific but the window/tray/floating-overlay/Compose-in-Swing mechanisms are generic desktop capabilities Template has none of.
portableItems.notes: Do NOT port as feature code. If desired, extract a thin generic core: (a) a SystemTray helper with mount()->Boolean + menu DSL + frame animation (strip the dictation/download-dot specifics), (b) a 'floating non-activating overlay window' helper = HudWindowBootstrap minus the DictationHudViewModel binding, keeping computeHudOrigin() and the NSPanel promotion (the NSPanel JNA shim itself lives in app:dictation:wire and would need relocating to a neutral module), (c) a 'Compose-in-JFrame' one-liner from CapNoteWindow. Medium-high effort because the bindTo()/wire dependencies must be severed. The pure computeHudOrigin() is directly portable on its own.

productSpecificExclusions.name: App.kt dictation boot wiring (BootDictationHotkeys / BootDesktopShell / BootDictationToastSurface) and DesktopShellBoot.jvm.kt
productSpecificExclusions.why: These mount the recording HUD, tray recording animation, cap-note window, global hotkey CGEventTap bridge, and dictation toast surface — entirely NoType's voice-dictation domain. The expect/actual seam SHAPE (a no-op-defaulted @Composable boot hook driven from per-platform main) is a reusable idea, but the bodies are product code. The PARAMETERIZED_STRING_KEYS / pre-resolved stringKeySnapshot machinery exists only to feed the dictation toast pipeline.

productSpecificExclusions.name: main.kt macOS mic + AX + dev-launch-identity wiring (NSMicrophoneUsageDescription, entitlements.plist, --add-opens for sun.lwawt.macosx, apple.awt.enableTemplateImages, java.net.http JLink module)
productSpecificExclusions.why: The microphone entitlement, accessibility-permission handling, NSWindow-reflection --add-opens flags, template-image tray hint, and on-device-model HTTP module are all needed only because NoType records audio, injects keystrokes, and downloads STT models. A generic template must not request mic/AX permissions or open sun.lwawt packages.

productSpecificExclusions.name: checkIosPlist / checkMacosPlist Gradle tasks (mic + UIBackgroundModes:audio plist lint)
productSpecificExclusions.why: These fail the build if NSMicrophoneUsageDescription / UIBackgroundModes:audio are missing — meaningful only for an audio-recording app. The MECHANISM (a Gradle task that lints a generated plist and wires into `check`) is a reusable scaffold worth noting, but the specific keys it enforces are product-specific.

productSpecificExclusions.name: nonWasmJsMain/jvmMain build.gradle dependencies on core:audio, core:ai(+deepgram/whispercpp/cleanup), app:dictation:*
productSpecificExclusions.why: The whole audio/transcription/AI-cleanup module graph and the kotlinx-datetime 0.6.x-compat pin (needed by okio-fakefilesystem in the dictation E2E harness) are NoType domain. Excluded wholesale.

productSpecificExclusions.name: Dropping the wasmJs target from composeApp
productSpecificExclusions.why: NoType removed wasmJs (no audio/dictation surface in browser) — visible as the missing wasmJsMain dir, the removed wasmJs{} block in build.gradle, and the README/docs switching the default run command from wasmJsBrowserDevelopmentRun to :composeApp:run. Template intentionally keeps wasmJs as a first-class target, so this removal must NOT be ported. (It's also why any expect/actual added in commonMain needs a wasmJs actual on port.)

productSpecificExclusions.name: README/GETTING-STARTED/ARCHITECTURE/iosApp/MainViewController/StringKeyResolver/AppLocale renames
productSpecificExclusions.why: All deltas in these are pure whitelabel output of setup.sh (template->notype, container names, default run command, package renames, dictation-specific StringKeys like ACCESSIBILITY_* / AUDIO_* / TRAY_MOUNT_FAILED_NOTICE). They are the RESULT of forking, not an improvement to port — porting the improved setup.sh subsumes them. CONTRIBUTING.md and the setup-project SKILL.md are byte-identical between repos (nothing to port).

risks: wasmJs ordering trap: NoType deleted the wasmJs composeApp target, so its commonMain `expect fun rememberSystemDarkTheme()` and `expect val allAppModules`/`sharedModule` have no wasmJs actuals. Template KEEPS wasmJs — every expect you port from NoType MUST get a wasmJs actual added or commonMain will not compile. Add wasmJs actuals first.

DI seam coupling: porting the expect/actual allAppModules requires Template to add a nonWasmJsMain source set and the applyDefaultHierarchyTemplate { common { group("nonWasmJs"){ withJvm(); withAndroidTarget(); withApple() } } } grouping in composeApp/build.gradle.kts, plus the iosMain/androidMain dependsOn(nonWasmJsMain) bridges. Do the build.gradle hierarchy change BEFORE moving DI to expect/actual, or the actuals won't resolve.

sharedModule today is a plain `val` in Template (commonMain only). Adding navigationModule is trivial if you keep it a plain val; only adopt the expect/actual sharedModule if you also need per-platform omission. Don't convert sharedModule to expect/actual just to add navigationModule — that's over-engineering for the generic baseline.

LocalTitleBarInset must move OUT of composeApp into the design-system module (it's a theme primitive). In NoType it lives in app:designsystem; place it in Template's TerminalTheme module, not composeApp, so feature screens can consume it without depending on the app shell.

setup.sh OLD_* constants are NoType-baked (template, application, etc. as the *new* names in places). When porting, the file must be reset so OLD_* = the template's own identifiers (template / com.m2f.template / application) and NEW_* come from the wizard prompts. Also re-verify whether the :server:distZip/distTar build exclude is still needed in Template (NoType describes it as a pre-existing template-level Gradle issue, so it probably is).

HudWindowBootstrap's NSPanel promotion calls setHudWindowFloating, which delegates to a NativeWindow JNA shim that currently lives in app:dictation:wire. To port the floating-overlay mechanism generically, that native shim must first be relocated to a neutral JVM module — otherwise the overlay helper drags in the dictation wire module. computeHudOrigin() (the pure placement math) has no such dependency and can be ported alone.

The tray/HUD/cap-note bootstraps reference AuroraTheme/AuroraText/AuroraButton/AuroraCard (NoType's design system). On port, swap to TerminalTheme equivalents. The TrayIconBootstrap copies brand hex colors locally with a 'must stay in sync with theme' comment — a deliberate decoupling, but a maintenance smell to flag.
